content: I've been using Lose-It back and forth. I lose weight then I gain it and I come back. Each time has gotten easier than the last, and this time I'm on pace to reach my goal with the assist of exercise. The app has a lot of customization and plenty of features making it easy to use while also providing a lot of features. Having premium is in my opinion worth it, as the extra tracking helped me to reduce frequent cravings. overall it's a 10/10 for me. Beats any diet or cook book I've used.

The voice entry option is GREAT in the app. A lot of times knowing I can just rattle off the things I remember eating for a meal and that it'll get them right (including amount) super quick is a big thing that has kept me going. I DO pay for the premium version, but it also feels worth it. They have a lifetime premium option as well if that is something you are looking for. This app has grown a LOT since I had started using it about 15 years ago on an iPod touch 😁

I have used this app for years and have the lifetimemembership. It has had some updates thst have had bugs, but they fix them pretty quickly. This app has helped me monitor my sugar, protein, fiber, cholesterol, and carbs to be healthier and discuss my habits with my doctors. I do wish it would do something when you hit a weight goal or a have a "good week". Other than that I really like this app.

I looked at a ton of tracking apps. This is the most complete and easiest to use app for tracking foods, weight, water, sleep etc. It's so easy to use. It includes ways to enter your food with voice or a photo and it's very reasonably priced. Since I installed it and chose the type of food plan that I want to follow, such as standard, keto, GLP-1 etc. it sends me tips and food ideas each day related to the plan I chose. Highly recommend! I love this app ❤️

Great app overall. Wish it had more stores that had certain items you'd be looking for, but I know it's impossible to get all the stores and all the foodsin the world. lol. If it doesn't have, let's say a restaurant item that you can't scan, I look for something comparable. Not an exact science but for the most part works with keeping you right around the calories the food may have been.

I've tried several different apps for food tracking and calorie counting and I like user friendly of this best. I find that snapping a photo of my food is probably 80% correct with what's on my plate and measurements. I have had no issues with scanning a barcode. I do wish there was more learning about different macros and how to eat better in each category.

This app uses generic clip art pictures for food and food types, probably to prevent copyright issues, but it will let you upload photos of your meals and keep them with your logged meal. The nutrition info for each food item is specific and editable, as products change over time. The meal summaries and daily summaries are easy to view. I really like the simple method to track water intake, also, and the other tracking options offered, including custom foods and recipes.

Great app, easy to find all kinds of food, can easily count your calories without paying for the Premium features. premium features include fasting timer, water intake, macro nutrients tracking, and much more little conveniences but nothing that's required to be successful in tracking calories. Also it's really cheap as well since they always have deals for it.

I have an older phone (Galaxy S10+), and the most recent update fixed a lot of the odd lagging/crashing that was an issue before, so I'm really happy about that. I really like the look of the new UI as well! Thank you for continuing to update and fix issues with the app. I love LoseIt, I've bought the lifetime membership and its absolutely worth it. I've been able to lose 10 pounds since December. 🎉 I'm going to keep doing my best!

so far so good! It's definitely a challenge sticking to 1,500 calories. But this app helps to keep track of them and I really like the food search option. I also love that you can scan the bar code on the food you're wanting to add and it will pull up all the details and nutritional information. Super easy to use so far and I already lost a pound!

easy to use. free version is great and I've used for years. but now that I'm consistently and obsessively tracking what I eat, I signed up for premium so I could plan ahead instead of just track the day. great food descriptions and very thorough plus you can scan upc e en with the free version, create recipes, add foods.

this is by far the best app I have used for calorie, counting and weight loss. It is incredibly easy to use and offers many ways to log the foods you are consuming. it's very easy to adjust quantities. it has many extra features that you can choose to use which allows you to customize it for your diet plan. I appreciate the carb counter. It can also track intermittent fasting easily. upgrading to ad-free was quite affordable and well worth it.

Just a brief review. I've been using this app for about ~1.5 years. It has a lot of features that make adding your calories quick or mindless compared to other ones ive used. I have tried just about every counter on the market. I have requested would be more organization for the user created recipes. I have a lot of recipes and it's just a giant list that is hard to navigate. The search option also seems faulty. Please consider adding folders or other options! Thanks for the great app.

I love this app. I started with the free version and tracked my meals for over 800 days. It's easy and accurate. I love all the pop up encouragement when you meet the 5lb loss increments. I've lost over 200 pounds already and just purchased the premium version to help me start tracking certain things, like protein, more in depth. If you are on the fence, get it.

This is the original review from 2020. Now in November 2025 the app is suddenly changed, it used to show your net carbs very distinctly and now they've changed the interface and it's not as intuitive anymore. 2020: Easy to use, I recommend getting the paid version as it will track your net carbs. Easy to scan barcodes if you eat processed foods. Recognizes seemingly thousands of different foods when you type it in!

I reported a bug using the email address under the Support option here. I got an automated reply saying the email was unmonitored. Maybe don't put that address there, then? The bug is now fixed: Add yesterday's meal feature, which I use for breakfast, is dropping selected items. It's been doing this for about a week.

This is a great app to use for tracking calories. Very easy to use, and almost anything you can eat is already pre-built into it. It can interface with Google Fit and calculate how many calories you have burned through exercise, and adjust your calorie budget accordingly. I started using this in March of 2025, and by September I had lost 50 pounds. This app changed my life. 100٪ recommend.

Awesome app with a few small rough edges. It has a few bugs in that doesn't always total up the nutrition info correctly if you use some units of measurement and it could really use a feature to export all your food with nutrition info, recipes, food log, and the data graphs into a PDF or HTML file so you can keep a record or share with a nutritionist/dietician. They also need a feature to report bugs or issues which I can't find anywhere.

Very easy to use app. There are so many precancerous options,which make entering what you ate,easy. I was able to look back,and see,areas I need to change,like cut carbs,increase protein intake. I am able to see daily,if I'm close to,under or over my goal for calorie intake. I also used the groups for support ,increased my activity and was able to lose weight easier ,with this app.

It's been about 6 years since I used a calorie counting app, (MFP is the reason I never tried another until now). I love this app so far. It's been a week and it's really helpful to be able to log foods so easily. I remember spending hours creating recipes on My Fitness Pal for simple foods. My one suggestion would be to add a few options. I'm breastfeeding and require a couple hundred extra calories a day due to this. I wish I could include that info to better customize my weight loss needs.

Recorded over 850 days, now its just part of the lifestyle. I haven't tried to lose weight in over a year, but I continue to use for tracking macronutrients and their balance. Works great for weight control too. The database is extensive, and the scan feature is spot on. A great feature I use is having a weekly report emailed to me that I add into an ongoing spreadsheet to better evaluate my progression and to identify any negative trend in my diet.

I have been using lose it for about 2 years to maintain my weight and have been successful. the app is easy to use and has a large data base of all kinds of foods and if any food isn't included, you can scan the QR code to get that food included in the data base. you can also enter food items with a voice option. there is also an option to include exercise calories to increase you daily allowance or not. it tracks weight, exercise, blood pressure and water intake. It is a great app.

Excellent app!! It makes counting calories suck less. It's easy to look up past foods and insert them or scan barcodes. A note feature would be nice, so that a note that says "Superbowl" reminds me why I GAINED weight that day! :-). The free version has upgrade ads, but they aren't TOO annoying. No other ads that I have noticed.

Seriously one of the best calorie tracking apps I've ever used. There's a free version with all the basic features and you can either type in your food item, scan a barcode, or even take a picture to get in the ballpark. Premium features include intermittent fasting, "high" days where it recalculates your calories to include treat days, and a bunch of other useful options. I would just add an option to turn off the predetermined amount of calories per meal. I save most of mine for dinner!

I am only a few days in, but so far I really like the app. My favorite feature is that I can scan everything, whether it's a barcode or a plate of food. So far every barcode has been recognized. It doesn't always recognize actual foods, but it's easy enough to do a search for something if it doesn't. I haven't tried all the features yet, but I think I am going to upgrade to premium once I get a wristband that tracks exercise. Once I have used more features I will update my review.

I've lost over 70 lbs so far thanks to the help of this app. If you stick to it and are honest with your logging, you will see real results. The biggest challenge for me is trying to figure out the calories for food that doesn't have them. It would be nice if they could add a feature that calculates the average calories of a specified food/meal. Currently, I search online, and the average the results myself.

Fighting weight all my life. More excersize and less calories is the only thing that works in the long run. And counting calories is the most difficult part of it. I've been using this app for several years. It's not difficult, and I find it quite accurate. I lost the weight I wanted and have sustained that by Keeping Up with the calorie counting.

I've been using lose it on and off for years. when I don't use it, I gain weight. when I do use it, I lose weight. Maybe one day I'll unlock the willpower to be able to eat proper portions without meticulously counting calories like an accountant but until that happens I'll be on here logging every gram. The app itself is pretty nice. they have a huge database of pretty much every food and in the rare cases they don't it's easy to add it through the camera. Highly recommend it!

For a free app, it's a great for tracking your dietary intake. It is user friendly. I like that you have the option of scanning barcodes. You can also, easily, alter dietary information if there is a discrepancy with an item that is already in their system. There are no annoying adds, just a small area at the top that asks you to go premium.

20lbs in 6 months! I was eating good food and exercising a lot, but no results. It was the quantity that was the issue!! Without this app, it is extremely difficult to KNOW (not think) how much I am actually eating. $20/year for premium features is not much and I actually bought it after I lost 17lbs! Now I am in maintenance and the premium features makes the app more continent.

Joined Weight Watchers and used their app. It was not user friendly and did not aid in keeping track of my daily intake. Calls to customer service were unsuccessful. This app is everything I've been looking for. It tracks everything for all meals ans snacks. Has a scanning feature that takes out all the research of how many calories in a serving. Highly Recommend.

Better than My Fitness! Bought yearly membership after logging my second meal! So much easier to log meals. I have an illness that requires 5-7 grams of sodium a day and at least 100 oz of water a day the app tracks it for me. Highly recommend! This app seems to have more food items also. There are many more reasons to get this app. These are just a few of them.

This app has decent UI; I've found it very easy to navigate.  The free version is serviceable if you're on a budget or like to save money, and the database of foods is comprehensive and makes logging meals a breeze.  Knowledge is power: having this app at my fingertips has instilled confidence and motivated me to stay consistent.  So far, I have lost 20 lbs. by simply sticking to a 1,833 calorie diet.  I'm never hungry, and I feel better than I have in years.  Give it a try!

I have never counted calories before but decided to focus on my health in retirement, including my weight. Lose It makes it easy: previous foods eaten are easily available, the bar code scanner makes it easy to add new foods, and many foods I eat out are also in the database. I love how I can subtract calories for exercise and how Lose It can get reports from my exercise app automatically. The periodic emails with dietary information have helped me focus more on what I am eating.

Food library is very expansive. Most chain restaurants and brands are in it. Bar code scanning is very nice. Adding my recipes make adding my home cooked meals a breeze. When I actively use it and consciously try to stay under my limits, I feel better and shed weight. It's also nice that it links with Fitbit, Garmin, and Google Fit. Would be nice if it supported Samsung Health.

I really enjoy this app , Its really easy to use and I love that it has a bar code scanner so I can scan my foods. Its a super fast way to record your calories. However I wish there was some way to record foods for the next day, as I have to prepare my breakfast lunch and snacks the night before for work. It would be so nice if I could record them for the next day this way I don't miss count my calories . Other then that it is a great app!

So far, after 3 weeks I've lost 10 lbs! So easy to use. Being able to scan your foods is one of the best features. I'd love to have some kind of "share" button to invite/find friends through Facebook more easily instead of using emails though. Still fantastic no matter what. Even if you aren't a Premium subscriber, this app works wonders! Thanks!!! ❤

I have been using this app now for over 900 days straight. I subscribe to the pro version and set goals for myself that are in line with my fitness journey. It has been an invaluable tool for me, and I have lost over 70 lb in just a few years! I'm also working to build strength and muscle for the long-term. This app has helped me understand my nutrient balance and I'm able to tweak and haul them over periods such as holidays.

As promised, I was going to change my rating if this app changed my view of it... And sure enough it did! I'm so glad I came across this app! It's much cheaper to subscribe than with MFP, which has ground to a halt for me. Boo! This app pretty much does it all, track cals, fasts, macros, and pretty much any health item you can imagine. I don't really see myself gonna back to MFP at this point ☝️

It's pretty much exactly what I wanted out of this app. It's got a great library of foods to look up. You can scan barcodes. It saves everything you've logged, so it's easy to find foods you've had before if you're eating them again. You can create recipes for something like a sandwich or Chipotle bowl, so it's easy to log those in 1 item in the future rather than looking up every ingredient every time. Tracks your macros and you can track your weight. And lots more, but I just ran out of space

I've tried several calorie counting apps, & Lose it is the best. It is the easiest to get your repeated foods, new foods, & to scan labels. I've lost weight with it several times in the last few years & just committed to the lifetime membership because I am committed to feeling this good the rest of my life! It all boils down to calories and making the right healthy choices. It's hard to stay under calories eating bad foods, so it's a learning process, but Lose it makes it easy! Thank you!

I'm loving this app so far! The database has been great and the user interface feels so much cleaner than other calorie counting apps I've tried. I also love how I'm able to easily change the unit of measurement (ounces, grams) to whatever I need and the program does all the conversions. It makes it so much easier to track and stay motivated. The app also seamlessly syncs my exercise data from Strava and adds my exercise calories. Highly recommend this app! :)

This app is extremely easy to use. I do not pay for anything to utilize, I have the basic version and it does what I need it to do. It only works if you are consistent and devote to logging everything you eat and the exercises that you do. You use your phone to scan UPC symbols on food packaging to simplify and speed up the logging process. There is also a line graph to show your progress with your weight to help you visualize your journey. There are also challenges you can do within the app

Love this app! The food database is up to date and accurate. (I compare it to the package info.) The food recording process takes seconds. The fitness tracking features are helpful. The dashboard gives a clear summary of where you are for the day. I use the app when I feel a need for a little extra discipline. It helps me to stay on track and lead a healthy life style. Would recommend highly.

It's very easy to use. You set your own goals. Make sure to hit the Done adding button at the bottom for each day. This will allow it to project your results. If you do well you hit your goal sooner. It really makes you pay attention to what & how much you eat. It's simple to add items too. You can search it, scan it or create it. It's quick to enter items you've already used and you can create a recipe that is multiple items for 1 meal.

unlike most apps which you have an option to get a subscription for It actually allows you to use it. It's really helpful if you want to kind of keep track of your food (food diary) and basic workouts. They have a huge database of foods, they even have foods in there from my local eateries. I personally only needed the basic stuff. But the paid option is really helpful for people who need extra support . It's not overly complicated and it helps you keep track of everything.

I love this APP!! It's very helpful in counting calories and so easy to use. It let's me scan the Barcode to input the nutrition label. It also lets me input my own recipes and then does the math as to how many calories are in each serving. I've lost 7 1/2 pounds so far! My whole family is using this app! 9 of us so far! My advise is to stick with it, even if you don't see results at first.

Best food tracking app,! Lost over 120 pounds in a year mainly through diet. I like the cute pictures next to the food; my ONLY complaint is when I can't find a picture for the food Im adding. I think I paid like $20 for this app, but I have definitely gotten more use out of it than that. Lots of positive, "supportive" people on here; some are a little too positive for me, but I'm a little weird. I wish there was a way to share pictures, but then there might be more creeps and attention seekers.

Used (free version) a couple years back, but fell through with it due to personal reasons. This worked amazingly and I never had trouble with it. When they didn't have an item I ate on their list, they allowed me to add it myself with the appropriate nutrition label. Definitely recommend for people who are looking to watch their calorie intake!

Awesome app! I have premium which is well worth the tiny fee. Love being able to track macros, exercise, etc. Love the database of foods - scanning a barcode or even adding a new food is super easy. Another favorite feature of mine is being able to create a "recipe" so if a meal/salad/snack i eat on a recurring basis always contains the same ingredients, all I have to do is create it once and then voila! I can just select the recipe instead of logging each ingredient every single time I make it

I've tried several tracking apps & I like this one a lot. I like the way it gives you options for caloric intake & then splits up your calorie allowance throughout the day. You can also track your fat, protein, & carb macros. So far, every food that I have looked up has been in the system, but it lets you create meals & add foods that are missing. Much less expensive than noom, but doesn't have all the cognitive behavioral therapy components which may be a pro or a con depending on your needs.

Love this app - I've used it off and on for years now (start back using it when I've put on a few pounds). Super easy to use - almost any food you can think of is an exact match. Graphs tracking weight loss and calories consumed are helpful. If you wish to count carbs or fat instead of calories you can change this over. Cheaper than the much hyped advertised app - it's really the same thing minus the pep talks and the calorie counter is easier to use.

This is the most amazing health app I've ever used. It's really good, and the food logging is the best ever. The food catalog is huge, and who would've thought you could point your camera at food to log it? I didn't, until I downloaded this app. And I'm using the free version, so you don't even have to pay to get most features! 10/10 would recommend 😀

I have previously used Noom and My Fitness Pal and neither was as easy to use as Lose It! I went with premium when given the half price option and I'm glad I did. I love the barcode scanner for entering information! I also like the setup for the meals. I can see which areas I need to focus on with the macro calculator. I'm much more mindful of what I'm eating now. I highly recommend this app!

This is the best app I have used for tracking calories. I have used My Fitness Pal, Weight Watchers, and numerous others. This app is easy to use. Easy to pull in repeat meals, easy to find restaurant foods, homemade foods, processed foods, easy to share recipes with friends. Connects with many devices and their apps. Love the user interface. Simply great!

Easy weight tracker!! No problems with Ads! Millions of foods in their database, plus if a food is not in there, which is rare, you can enter it manually yourself! Tracks your carbs, proteins, fats, etc of what you've eaten in a day in easy pie chart form. Tracks any exercise and water consumed also. I love that it tells me the date I will reach my goal weight, as long as i continue to monitor my food intake through the app. It makes me 100% accountable for my food choices. LOVE! <3

My issue was resolved after posting a fairly negative review and having customer service reach out to me. So overall I'm happy with the fact that the app is working again and happy with how customer service continue to reach out to offer support and assistance. This has been the best app I've used for calorie counting and Intermittent fasting too.

This is the best app to develop safe habits around diet and exercise. By setting a daily caloric budget and tracking every food and drink you ingest, you are forced to eat healthy, which means you learn to appreciate which foods have unnecessary and excessive calories. Soon, you learn to avoid them, if not despise them. This is the essence of eating habit change versus "a diet." When the power of eating habits is combined with trade-offs for exercise, the result is a complete lifestyle change. Once you achieve a lifestyle change, you never need to go on a diet (or anything like it) again! This is the safest, healthiest and most educational life-changing application there is.

No quick fix, no trendy diet, no quick uptick in working out, no expensive shakes or prepacked meals. I am proud to say that in the last 2 months I have lost 15 pounds, mostly by journaling my food intake, every crumb, in the Lose It app. The app keeps me in line. The barcode scanner in it is remarkably accurate. The algorithms in the app gave me a calorie target to fit my loss target and it has been SPOT ON. Use this app, put EVERYTHING in there, don't cheat yourself and you WILL lose weight!

I love this tool. If you have a difficult time tracking your food intake, this app simplifies your efforts. It has a variety of brand names to select from, it gives you a My Food Choice to track the foods you eat, a place to save your favorite meals. It tracks your exercise and gives extra calories for your effort. It has much more. Check it out.

I'm extremely impressed with this app. It has extensive options reaching into many areas of health though can easily track single things like calories, weight, foods, steps, measurements, blood pressure, sugar, carbs, sleep hours, etc. The app did a good job of starting small until I was ready to dig down into the options. Scanning bar codes is awesome. I'm about to enter/save my meals for easy entry of entire meals. There's included reviewed articles and social support too if you're into that.

This is a great tracking app! It's very simple - you set your goals and it tells you how many calories you need to consume to reach your goals. It also gives guides as to how to split up the amount of fats, carbs, and protein you require. There's a large database of food/drink items and you also the ability to scan an item in or manually enter an item if it's not in the database. Definitely helps to keep me honest and on target.

This app is amazing and perfect for what anyone needs to lose weight. There's no food I've found so far that isn't in their database of foods. It's very accurate, sends reminders to track meals, and allows you to set goals and keep track of other nutritional information. While there is a premium you can pay for that provides more features, the free mode gives you more than enough without making you feel like you have to buy the premium. 9/10 amazing app. I've already lost 18 pounds in a little over a month

33 pounds down and working on gaining muscle! I have used the app for about 2 years now and have seen constant improvements. For the most part, it is easy to use and helps me stay on track. I would like to see the "Plate Reports" on mobile to make sure I am getting enough veggies, but not a big deal. I can't imagine my health journey without this app.

Easy to use and addictive. Once you build a profile, the App pretty much does the rest. Just can scan or type in foods to find nutritional content, add appreciate quantity and save. The true value of App is that it helps you evaluate your food choices and eliminates absent minded eating. I have only been using it for a week but I'm losing weight at a sustainable rate and I'm never hungry. I'm eating a lot more vegetables and much fewer processed foods.

I've had this app for many years and use it often, currently I use it daily. It's amazing to connect with my Garmin smartwatch so it automatically pulls in my exercise data. I also love that they've added additional goals beside just losing or maintaining weight. Since I've started weight training for the first time I love being able to track my body measurements instead of solely the weight on the scale.

I have lost 36 pounds so far and love the free version of this app. It is easy to scan barcodes and track calories. I love the badge feature. It would be cool if you could use benchmarking data to compare my weightloss with similar women of age and height. Or maybe a few success stories of women who have lost weight and how they did it, in a separate tab.

This app is amazing if you want to become more mindful about what you eat. I dont have premium, however with what the app does offer for free, I have been able to to see for myself what has been entering my body not just in terms of carbs, but fats, and proteins as well, giving me the awerness I need to make better choices when it comes to picking out meals. I've realized that weight loss comes from an inner drive, and this app helps give me the mindfulness I need to get there.

I gave this app a 5 star rating because you have the option to buy premium with special features, but you can do all of the basic functions without spending a dime! I am able to track my calories, exercise, and weight, which was the key reason I was looking for a calorie counting app. it is also good at keeping track of your goals and easily showing you your progress!

When I first started I used the free version, and I liked it, so I committed to premium and here I am on a 700 and some day streak. I like the variety of options it allows in food management diets, the fact that I won't lose my streak if I forget to click the finalize my day switch before midnight, the weight lose comparisons, the ability to make recipes and add anything new to the system if it's not already there.

I like Lose It compared to other apps because their foods list is extensive. I have yet to find something that I have eaten that has not been on their list - and it includes branded manufacturers and many restaurants. Additionally, I love that it breaks down my meal into the summarized nutrients so I can see how I balanced my proteins with my carbs, etc. If you are someone who doesn't need or want a coach, but you want a place to keep track of your exercise and eating habits - this is for you!

This app is super easy to use for tracking all your health metrics. Recently I decided to take control of my health, I have High Blood pressure, am overweight, out of shape and not getting any younger. I am able to set goals for multiple health components, not just weight. When I meet with my PCP I will be able to show her a months worth of complete data and we can better partner on my long term goals for achieving a healthy lifestyle and not just focus on "losing weight ". Highly recommend.

I have to praise the devs and support. I was having the "done logging" issue as well and I sent them an email about it; they gave me a month of premium so it would work again and detailed what they thought the issue could be. Then they asked to make sure that the button worked in premium and now they actually got back to me to tell me that they pushed the "done logging" fix through. I really wasn't expecting that kind of support!

After trying out Lifesum, MyFitnessPal, and SparkPeople (all really good apps), I've found this one to be the winner. The interface is nice to look at, there are a lot of really helpful features, and the number of food items in its food item bank is robust. Adding a few more exercise options to the app couldn't hurt, but this is still my top-choice food tracker, for sure.

I'm literally crying right now! I lost 18 lbs in the last 3 months using this app!!! I thought there was something wrong with me forever and I was about to give up and just be the fat auntie. The premium feature that tells you what foods are keeping you on track has really been so helpful, it encourages me to log everything so the stats can be accurate. I've never stuck with weight loss this long! I highly recommend this app!!! Be sure to use a food scale for accurate measurement

Lost 30 pounds in 3 months. Cut insulin in half. Doctor thrilled at progress. Wrote down app info to check it out. Feel my overall health is better. I have learned so much, and spend my calories more thoughtfully. The app makes it easy to make my choices by watching my fat, protein and carbs. I have become more aware of the amount of sodium i consume. What an eye opener! So happy with this app, and the comunity chats are so inspiring.

This is great app for tracking your food intake, exercise, weights, and other health measures. I especially like how many foods you search for of common restaurants and food services are already included. It takes out a lot of the work for you. I set a goal to lose 12 lbs. and I am well on my way thanks to Lose It! I've not tried other popular apps like MyFitness Pal, but I am pretty sure it would be comparable, if not better. I think it has more food listings to search from.

Best out there. The free certain is great by itself, but it's worth it to subscribe, and affordable. Easiest to use, and will track carbs, calories, fiber, fat grams, etc .. Figures out your calorie needs to lose weight at a rate your comfortable with, this app does all the work for you. Will configure in exercise if that's your preference, track your sleep, water, and macros. Also has groups to join and challenges if you're a social butterfly. I can't say enough about this app. 😊☺️

I've been using this app to count calories since 12/27/23 and I have to say... I love it! I paid for the premium version so I could have full access to features and it does not disappoint. I have lost 32.8 pounds since i started using this app, and I really think it has kept me accountable in many ways. In addition to counting calories, I track my weight and log exercises. Definitely worth the download, whether you choose to pay for premium or not.

Easy to find foods and to use them on a daily basis since it remembers what you used often. Also easy to put in exercise and to keep track of your weight. I really like the fact that anyone can use this app and it works well. If there is any downside to the free app is that pretty much every day you have at least one or two times and add pop up but I just hit no thanks and go on to my work. They want you to buy the paid one.

This app did help me out with counting and logging the calories. It's an encouragement and challenge to stay within ur calorie budget. I did lose almost 40 pounds with calorie count. Lose it rocks and also I recommended it to a lot of friends and I still recommend it. The food search has different kinds of foods from all around the world. U can even scan the barcode label to log the food ( much easier ). U can keep a track of ur weight by logging it everyday. Also u can log BP, sugar levels etc

This is a good app for dieting. Similar to the Weight Watchers app minus the monthly fee. There are some premium features here but it works well for free. I like that it makes it easy to add walking as exercise and deduct it from your day. I have gone from 200 to 190 lbs with this app and from quarantine 210 lbs, but im back to using it. If calorie counting works for you, this is great for the long haul. If not, try other solutions for weight loss before passing blame.

It works! I started weighing in at 274 pounds back in the Summer of 2023, as of the writing of this review, I now weigh 166. I would leave a screenshot showing my log in the app if I could. I counted calories while maintaining an intermittent fasting schedule, and I like to walk. There was a learning curve and adjustment period the first month, but scanning snacks and looking up meals are a breeze once you get used to it.

Good overall but there are frustrating and QoL things that I would like to see fixed or added. The frustrating part is that when trying to enter a meal, it's difficult to edit the items in the meal. if I choose a meal I had before but had different amounts, I can't change the amount in the meal as it adds another element to the meal as a separate item. QoL, would like to see my recommended macro nutrition goals w/values Ive had

I paid for premium on 3 apps and then cancelled two of them after deciding on this one. I've been using Lose It for over a month now. The calorie counter is superior to any I've used before. I also like how you can join or read social posts. The price is better than the other apps as well. I've lost over 10 pounds so far. It has an exercise tracker that is great for me but it might be too basic for more serious athlete types.

I was recommended MyFitnessPal by my nutritionist but I was not able to make an account. I looked up come competitors and I found LooseIt! I am very grateful that I did because this app is fantastic. I did not mind paying the $40 for the yearly subscription because I have been using this thing daily. The food database and the exercise database are amazing. It also allows you to make your own recipes and exercises to calculate things more in tune to your personal day-to-day. This app is awesome!

Compared to my old subscription tracker app, Lose It! has more foods (with complete nutrition info) on record to choose from, the scanner works SO well!, and it's more comprehensive, inclusive, catered to me & my goals, and can sync with my fitness app! --for half the price of my old app!! I'm such a happy Lose It! convert that I'll be singing its praises to all my friends & family!

I'm 5'9" male and went from 180 lbs to 160 lbs with this app. It took me a while to really learn how to accurately count my calories, I recommend getting a kitchen scale. But once I got myself in a groove, the pounds just fell off. I also recommend weighing yourself everyday in the morning (don't get discouraged when the weight goes up on some days!) and getting a fitness watch so you can know how mamy you're burning. Weight loss is a simple matter of math: burn more than you eat.

This app has been invaluable in my weight loss journey. I've lost 8 lbs in less than 3 months! All my clothes fit comfortable again & at 60, I'm back to what I weighed in my 30s! This app is great whether you eat premade or homemade! I cook everything from scratch & so many foods in the app that have calorie counts listed from recipes I use - or they're really close. You can also add your own to the database. Love being able to log exercise & the weight graph showing my progress! Free version!❤️

This was the best of 3 apps I tried. Still could use some fine tuning for ease of use but once you are used to it, not bad. The others were insistent on taking a subscription but this one lets you try it. I wish it would have a default page of adding food because that is the page used the most. The food search is pretty versatile and brings up a variety of options.

Lose It! is an awesome app! I've been using it for quite awhile now and, even though it is a calorie tracker like so many, I love the inclusion of the macronutrients. I like seeing my calories broken down into those components. Plus, I love the little food icons!! They're so cute and they make me happy! And the food database is ASTOUNDING! There are so many options and so many foods, plus you can always add your own if it doesn't exist! Thank you so much, devs! I don't think I'll ever stop!

It comes down to discipline, no mater what the app tells you. If you eat less calories then you burn - you will lose weight. With that being said, this app was awesome in helping me identify my bad eating habits and in turn, eliminate them. I'm currently at 197 from 230lbs (6ft male). The app didn't make me lose the weight, but it was an excellent log. Be honest with yourself and you will see results.

Overall the app is great, It's just when creating your own exercise it's very limited (i.e. I can only set minutes or hours, not reps.. The selection excludes actual exercises) I do have the free version, if that has anything to do with it lol. Edit: I realized that some of the exercises are grouped (Core, Resistance, etc.).. which works for me. But I would still like to create exercises with reps 🫡..

I love this app! It's easy to use, accurately records my food/ calorie intake, including restaurant meals, grocery items through bar code scanning, and home cooked meals. The daily reminders are a fun way to keep me on track. You can also record your activities and workouts, subtracting those calories from your daily total, and I appreciate how it provides me with ongoing calorie totals so I can budget my food throughout the day.

I've been using this app for 911 days as of the day of this review. It's been a great way to track calories and exercise. I like the simplicity of the app. The support was great when I brought a bug to them. They patched it in a week. I have a paid subscription and the lifetime purchase pushing is a bit over-the-top, but that's about my only objection. I'd like to see a better way to move a meal to another day for those times I enter a meal into the wrong day. Overall, great app!

Exactly What I Was Looking For. This app has set me up a plan based on my height and weight and my goals for losing the weight. Additionally the app has given me control over how fast I chose to lose the weight to create realistic expectations. I loved how the app allows you to scan barcodes from food items to get accurate calorie counting. I'm on Day and I feel much better than when I started! You'll be amazed of how many mindless calories we consume on a daily basis.

great app. I needed a couple years to try getting motivated to use it. But in a about 2½ months I've lost 38 pounds. the app is great for tracking my calories intake and if i can control my hunger. I can eat 300-1000 less calories below budget per day so ive been able to lose 3-4 pounds on some weeks. and the premium benefits are pretty good lots of stuff. i don't use most of it but definitely worth the price. also if you time it right you can get lifetime for fairly cheap.

This app is stellar! Even the free version is really good. It contains caloric counts for many many foods (you can also scan bar codes) and drills down to your daily fat, protein, and carb intake. The log looks really clean: you see how many calories you can take in, how many you've used up, and whether you're over or under. You get a daily and weekly overview and see how you progress to your goal. Afs are very unobtrusive. I'll go for the paid version to help these developers.

I've used Lose It! On and off for several years now. When I faithfully log, I lose weight. I really enjoy being able to just scan bar codes and not have to worry about logging every detail. Upgrading to the premium is worth the buy as well. It allows me to create recipes so if my family has a favorite soup or casserole dish, I create a recipe the first time. After that, I just click on the recipe and log how many servings I ate. My eldest son now uses the app.

So far its working great! This is my first day but it seems to have all foods even the stuff I order from optavia show up. Its a great way to keep track of exactly what your eating. You think oh this little bit of this or that here and there aren't going to hurt you. But when you can add it all up...... you realize how many calories you are actually eating. Definitely keeps me on track and aware of how much I'm eating. Plus its super easy to use.

I really do love this app, it's nice and simple for the most part. I paid for the Lifetime so you know I'm a fan. However, there are some issues. There are A LOT of foods that just aren't on here so most days I'm straight up making new ones to then track. But if I take more than 30 seconds to create the food item, after I click done all the data is reset and I have to restart. This also happens when trying to make recipes (which is one of the best capabilities of the app). I would love a fix. 🙏

I like the app and it's easy to use but you guys broke something recently. Yesterday my steps recorded no problem, today they won't record, not even manually. They usually record through health connect but it's showing like 200 steps when I have over 10k. I tried to manually record them but it won't accept that either. It's still recording workouts, though.

$80 a year is too expensive for apps like this, luckily the free version works super well so you don't need to pay. Other free apps work well too, but this one's my favorite. They offered 50% off but I didn't take it, though im not sure how $50 is half of $80. A week later they gave 70% off, so I jumped on that. Full $80? No way. Premium's customizations are useful due my tracking needs. Love the scanning feature, easy to add items, though so much is already in the database.

it's great. this recent update move the "Done Logging" button to under the last meal. I liked it better in the former spot, way more convenient. not a fan of the new spot. that aside the majority of meals are accurate, I've lost roughly 40 pounds since using it. if it doesn't have it then I get to do the math. I do everything manually, I'm not much for using AI. I should try it sometime, but I'm faster.

I've been using this app for the last like two months or so. It's very helpful in keeping track of your calories burned & gained. I also like that you can track fasting. One reason I gave it 4 stars, is cos it would be great if y'all would add the option to connect our samsung health data on this app automatically. I have samsung & i have to manually input steps/exercises done on this app; that was recorded automatically through my watch/phone. Please add that option outside of Google fit etc.

I like the app, but there is some issue with upgrading to premium and then lifetime ..that doesn't really work? or it doesn't keep me logged in? I have had it happen multiple times that I've been logging food for months and I get tired of the ads for lifetime (but I upgraded!) and I see that I can't edit my profile or anything else. I get "An Error Occurred Lose It! was unable to load the required data. Please make sure that you have a network connection and try again."

Much more accurate than BitePal. I'm on a calorie deficit, and I was using BitePal, but the calories weren't accurate. This app is much more accurate, and it counts the calories for you. You type in the food, and it counts the calories. I love the recipe button. You just put your ingredients in, it counts the calories, and tells you how much one serving is. My only critique is that I also use KeepHealth+, and I want to sync my steps into Lose It!

Update: Still a nice app to manually track diet intake. Upped to 4 stars for the recent change made. The app is slow when going/returning to the "log". It seems to be related to the ad service they use. There's a 15 second delay before it recognizes any interaction attempts (taps). The list of foods the app provides are usually *incorrect* for the nutritional info. You can create your recipes and foods to track nutrition details. Any bells and whistles are only available in the premium version.

Great app & features, but needs tweaking. Food logging pg is too busy with suggestions. When selecting how much food you ate, there are many items that only give you the option of a serving or grams. I dont always know how much food is in 1 serving, nor am I familiar with grams for food. I'd like to see more options like cups, etc. It also doesn't let you log weight for multiple days at one time if you miss logging - you have to do each day separately. The app is destined for greatness, though!

This app is easy to navigate and use. Pros: Easy navigation, large data base of different foods including ethnic foods. Cons: The scanner doesn't work even after logging a complaint that was never resolved. Suggestion to the creators: Can we get a search bar on the discover feed section to search for key words that will bring up articles on specific things we would like to read up on to help us on our journeys?

There needs to be an option to turn off automatically setting all future day foods as "planned." It's truly annoying to plan your meals ahead, then have to open every individual food on the log and change that setting. This is a big annoyance for those of us who are good at planning. Otherwise, app is very good

Please return the arrow back to the calendar! they just removed the arrow to go back to yesterday. why??? why remove the arrow???now I have to open a calendar, which takes like ten seconds, then find yesterday select and enter. it used to have an arrow. forgot to enter some last thing from yesterday? work an unusual schedule? Too bad, you have to jump hoops thanks to this innovative update that makes it more inconvenient. while that happens you may have to deal with disruptive ads.

Upgrading to premium is broken. there seems to only be one way to do this - through the app. Which would be fine if there was a way to edit creds instead of the app locking in a default to use the google account associated with my phone. Otherwise giving Lose It my money is an exercise in aggrevation. Also, as many others have noted, I liked Lose It vs other apps like MyFitnessPal becsuse it DIDN'T have all the extra unecessary garbage. Lately they seem to be going in the wrong direction.

I have been using this app for 9 years on & off, and I love that it retains all that data. The app has evolved over the years to include tracking of nutrition, specific diet plan and eating plans, ability to create your own recipes, and scanning bar codes for easy logging, in addition to weight tracking, water intake and excercise. I have found it worth it to me to subscribe to premium. my only issue right now is I can't update my profile. can I get some help? getting error.

Wonderful app for tracking your diet and exercise and it's mostly free! You can upgrade for a fee, but can really get the gist of what it can offer for free. One problem is that some of the foods pre installed or added by participants have incorrect nutritional information. You have the option to add foods and edit them, but sometimes it doesn't save accurately. Be aware.

Lose it has really helped me. I have lost over 10 lbs. I would love to have the premium subscription, but I can't afford all of it at once. If they broke it up into 2 payments, I would do that. Adding a new recipe is confusing and I if I mess up, I have to start all over, I can't delete an ingredient after I save it. Also the weight loss configuration does not account for body frame.

I used this in the past and he tried others and I always end up going back to this app. I especially like the feature where it gives the grams of protein, carbs, and fats for the day. I hope they continue to grow their food database and hopefully there's an easier way to log food if you primarily cook at home (from recipes). But overall my favorite app to use for tracking food.

The only thing that could make this better for me is to make it to where both of my devices with the same account are updated to match each other. When at work or out, I use my phone, when at home, I use my reading tablet. Sucks that if there is some issue it doesn't update between devices unless I uninstall and reinstall it. Doesn't happen too often, but often enough to be an annoyance. If there was a way to just sign out and sign back that'd be awesome, but there isn't...

This app is very user friendly. It's helped me keep better track of what I'm eating and when I exercise. I've lost 25 lbs in 2 months. I wish the premium version offered a discounted trial, or billed monthly. So far I'm still on the free version. Update: after I hit the 25 lb mark, and especially after the 50 lb mark, there were no more badges to earn. It's been very disappointing. That was a big part of the motivation and fun.

Update: Trying to unsubscribe from the yearly fee crashes the app. (Did it via desktop website) I did the yearly program which unlocks a lot of insightful things. I like that it tracks progress, goals, and shows you calorie counts, but I am not a fitness person so I may be missing features or where it could be improved. I'm just taking the app at face value & following its suggestions. One thing that's cool is that you can just scan UPC codes of the food container and add it to your meal.

I like how when I search for an item in My Foods that it's ordered by most recent. Search function is also a lot more functional. On the other hand, with my iPhone I could hit the edit button and easily move items around when logging my meals, along with swiping to delete an item. Where's the option on Android? There also seems to be a bug: If I accidentally select or add an item and then unselect it, it will still say that I've had that item recently, and now I can't see the real last date.

I've been using this app for a week now, and I'm actually seeing results with very little to no exercise. The app can get a bit glitchy, but it is easy to use and has a huge library of foods that you only have to scan the barcode on the product to enter. It honestly feels like a game to me where my calorie budget is my in-game currency that I'm using to "purchase" my meals. I'm loving this app!

I haven't been using this app for very long - so I'll try to update once I continue. However, the app is very easy to use and has a smooth interface. Even without the premium version, you can see your predicted goals, and the percentages of carbs, fats, and proteins in your food. In setup, it will ask if you want to look better vs feel better, or what's more important to you in your food. The only reason I gave four stars and not five is because I havent been on the app for long. 💖💫😊

Best app I've found for calorie tracking. It makes it easy enough to do that I don't have trouble coming back to it. My biggest gripe: while it says you can set your own goals - maintaining weight, building muscle - it's still 100% focused on weight loss. I'm underweight and my goal is to be above a certain weight and cal count, but it tells me I've reached my goal when I record my weight because it's under my goal. When I'm over my calorie minimum, it turns red; when under, it congratulates me.

I like the app, it does exactly what I needed which was log my food and track calories. I gave it a 4 because the premium portion you can pay for is not worth the amount they charge. I tried the free with the ads and the premium and I didn't utilize the extra micronutrients tracking because I didn't need all that but someone else might.

This app works extremely well for keeping on track with my weight loss goal. It's easy to use and has a robust catalog of foods in the search tool. My one "complaint" is regarding the feature of having extea calories budgeted to certain days of the week. I feel that the extra calories would be more useful if they had a separate tracker. For example, if I want to alot myself 700 floating calories to use on any given week I could apply those to the "extra bucket" on any given day that works best.

After scanning an item, it may be incorrect. I scanned beef, seaweed snacks, keto flour, and other food items, and the calorie counts were all incorrect. The keto flour says 1/4 of a cup is 80 calories on the bag, yet the app tells me it's 110 for the same amount. The beef I bought says it's 190 calories for four ounces, but the app says three ounces. Net carb calculator works and it's a good way to count calories. Better than MyFitnessPal. But needs some adjustments.

It's a helpful everyday tool for calorie tracking. I like that you can personalize your calorie goal and track proteins, carbs, and fat. It could also potentially be helpful for maintaining or gaining healthy weight. ⭐One thing I wish they would fix though is sometimes I will see two listings of the same exact food or drink that have different calorie/nutrition info, but are both verified for correctness. This is conflicting info and makes the source less trustworthy!

Easy to add, although a bowl of cereal, for example, can take a while, since each ingredient is a separate line. Excercise calories burned may or may not be legit, but judge for yourself and i just adjust as needed. There are a few things that you can do online but not in the app, so check out all the settings on both.

Pretty sweet app. Very similar to MyFitnessPal but without the extreme data usage. Food list has very few erroneous entries as compared to competitors. Integrates with Google Fit and Strava. Few suggestions for improvement: - Need the ability to log water ounces by user-specified number instead of 8 ounce increments - Add ability for a family or group of friends to create a private group and share their progress with each other. The best way to stay fit and healthy is with accountability from a friend (not a random stranger online). - Consider keeping the discount price at $30 ($40 is too much) or even dropping it to $20 per year. If you add the private group option above, it will definitely be an incentive for people to pay for the app.

Consistency is key. The app has cute little reminders to keep you on track. Honestly writing down what I'm eating on a daily basis has been the key of my weight loss. sometimes just knowing that you will have to add something to your tracker will keep you from eating something that is not healthy for you. I got the premium version for the ability to track in advance because if I am planning on eating a certain thing I can budget for that. I've been eating the calories recommended and have not been hungry. it's up to you and how dedicated you are to your own health but this is a very helpful tool.

I am finding it useful, except it doesn't send calories correctly when connected to Google Fit...It only sends the calories of the first food item of the day, and stops until the following day, instead of sending the total when you are done with the day. **edit: calories are sending after every item now, but if you make changes to (or remove) an item, it can't update Fit correctly.

I really like this app, the only thing that would make it better is if we could "favorite" foods to come back later to log them instead of having to search them every single time. Being able to scan the label is nice if they have them, but when we have leftovers, it sucks having to search the meal to log it every single time or even keep the barcode to scan it just to log the leftover until they are all eaten. I pay for premium, so this would be an awesome addition to premium membership.

I like this app. Do I love it? No. But it's adequate for starting out on my health journey for now. I use the free version, which allows me to track my exercise, weight, what I eat (calorie count) and also see my daily food intake divided into fat, protein, and carbs. The "upgrade to unlock" parts are a little in your face, as well as the frequent "sales" for premium, etc. It would be nice to not have the premium features on your dashboard. But overall it's doing what I need.

I have been using this app for a little over a week now and I love it. I upgraded to premium 3 days in and am glad I did. It is very easy to track calories, macronutrients and sodium, as well as my weight and blood pressure. Extensive food bank with many national restaurants and store brands, the ability to scan bar codes and manually enter nutrition info to create foods and recipes. It loses one star b/c the "scan your food" function needs work...it identified pork tenderloin as French toast!

I love this app! I would have given it 5 stars because it does really meet all my needs except: 1. It doesn't allow to move a food item into a different meal rather than having to delete it, then add it to the correct meal. 2. It would be helpful to edit personally created recipes. For example: any recipes that I created have to be recreated if I need to edit ingredients. Honestly, these are features I'd like to see added in new editions of the app. Hopefully they read customer comments! ;-)

i like that it has a large database of foods. and to use the basic functions you dont have to pay. I do wish some of the foods had more options of measurements. i mostly use cups and tablespoons so ounces and grams dont really help me. Also, there needs to be a way to edit or delete recipes. After seeing the calories on some of the things i eat ive been trying to modify but i have to make a whole new recipe rather than edit one i already have.

I've been using the app consistently for a month and I've lost 13lbs (and still going). Counting calories is the only consistent form of "dieting" that has worked for me so far. I'm not even exercising! The scanning of the barcodes is a little annoying and measuring everything out, but it's definitely worth it. Definitely worth a try on the app.

Writing down my meals daily has been very motivating. I like Lose it because it is easy to search for the nutritional value of most of my meals, however the nutrition value of many food items are not complete or have not been verified by Lose it. So I find myself checking for myself. Also more instructions are needed for recording your meals. You eventually figure it out, but you end up using the Save feature erroneously at first. Still, it is a very good app for weight loss goals.

Nice calorie tracking app with a bunch of prefilled foods. The ability to add your own recipes is very nice. There is no way to manually lower your max daily calories, its figured out by how much you want to lose, with the max being 2lbs a week, but even if you know you need less calories you cannot change it. The water intake tracker doesn't pull from the food logger. For example if I add a cup of tea for lunch, I have to add 8oz to the water tracker.

I have the base app and it works perfectly for helping me track my calories and stay on track. I'm down 12 pounds in three weeks while allowing myself a weekly cheat day. I love the bar code scanning feature and the ability to lookup food from popular restaurants. I have one gripe... I added an exercise (weightlifting) from the list of exercises and now when try to add another exercise my only option is is weightlifting, I can't get back to the full list (no option to remove filter).

Came back to this app after a hiatus to have a baby. Now I can't access any of the analysis on my DNA, and my reports are no longer getting emailed to me. I'm pretty disappointed with my year subscription, and I won't make that mistake again. - edit: Their help desk was very responsive and was able to correct the problem I identified. I hope they keep making strides to improve and maintain the service!

This really does help me stay accountable. Very easy to use. Saves meals for easy reuse (rather than entering each item all over again). The bar code scanner works great. They have most restaurants/brand name foods. Lets you track macros. My only wish is that I could meal plan my days for a week at a time without paying for premium as the other features arent that exciting for me. Otherwise love it.

Love this app!! I have tried other apps, but this is the best I've tried. Very easy to use. Love that I can just log quick calories if I don't want to enter a new food. The database for restaurant foods seems to be pretty accurate. I would have rated it a 5 a couple of weeks ago, but they took away one of my favorites features, which was the quantity "spin" feature. Now you have to enter fractions on your own. So now if I eat 3/8ths of something, I have to figure out for myself what that is to enter. Sounds like a silly complaint, but fractions have never been my strength. Other than this one little thing, it's the best app around!!!

So far I've lost over 100 lbs. I'm on a CC diet ("counting calories"). Couldn't have done it without Lose It. I only have one objection and one suggestion! Objection: I'm an android user (as I'm sure many users are) and I can't transfer a posted meals to a different date. That means I end up posting it twice then deleting the post on the date I don't want. It's time consuming. Suggestion: I want to be able to access my notes with a search option so I can refer to them.

Personally, this works great for me because of what habits I needed to chnage to lose weight. if you're like me, you often binge without looking to see what's in the food, and them try to cut portions but only estimate. With this app, I was able to see what's in my food, how much I should eat for my desired weight loss, and it worked out great, currently have lost 4 pounds. However, for someone looking into tracking every variable of their health, this is not the fitness app for you.

helpful app to help you keep track of all your calories during the day. it has been very helpful so far, ive already lost 2 pounds. I did just have a small bug in the app though, i tried to add a meal on sunday morning, but any time i would add it, it would add it to the day before. so now its showing my saturday calories as if i ate too much and i cant fix it, nor will it add the meal to todays counter. im going to try to uninstall it and reinstall it to see if it fixes it.

I like this app, but it is my second choice if I could only choose one. I use 2 and this app has a more extensive library. Custom recipes are easy to create. Navigation isn't hard but could be more convenient. I have a yearly subscription but I do wish they had recipes to recommend for the diet you decide to choose to better direct a user. Plus, any additional information is an additional cost which I feel, should be included to help someone on their journey.

As a premium user, this app has been great for logging, tracking and storing my homemade recipes over the past nearly 10 years. I do think it lacks some features. I wish it would give me the option to have my calories adjust based on over or under eating in days prior, and sometimes the display is hard to navigate on the goals tab, but aside from that it does just what I need it to do.

Love the app, have used it for years. It's the nicest user interface among the calorie trackers out there. My only wish is that it had a tracking plan for gaining weight as well (I know, blasphemy to the name of the app). When trying to put on weight, everything turns red and looks like it's shaming you. Would love the option to set different calorie goals above maintenance.

Tracking caloric intake is easy with the free app. Accepted my goal weight and told me how many calories a day to lose the weight in two years. Then it has an extensive list of foods, some by brand, with calories. Have to be careful bcz you/I can add foods and some of the calories are way off. Then it remembers my foods, and it remembers my meals, so i can pick them off a list instead of recreating everytime. And I can put in exercise and it subtracts calories.

It's pretty solid. I enjoyed it as a free user. Because it didn't feel like I was being abused, unlike MyFitnessPal, and the price was fair I decided to go premium. I'd still strongly recommend the all for those that are looking for calorie tracking on a budget. That said they could make the app even better if they would add a bit of case based reasoning when I'm adding foods to my diary. I'd like the app to be smart enough to know that I prefer a certain type of measurement for certain foods.

I have used this app on and off for a few years now and have lately purchased the yearly membership in order to get motivated to really start paying closer attention to what I consume. However, I do have one request. I wish there was a section of the app entirely for scanning labels. Instead of waiting to scan a label when I go to eat, it would be nice to go to a "Scanned Foods" section when I get home from the grocery store, this way it is easier to quickly log meals.

I've been a premium member for a few years. I bought the Black Friday special online for $15/year. I appreciate that this app has great features for a reasonable premium price, unlike others I could name. The developers seem to listen to member feedback and are always making improvements. We finally got an option to select different calories for different days of the week. And they removed the 800 calorie minimum. Now I can finally set up my 4:3 calorie budget!! I'm so happy!!!

I like this app. It's very easy to use and it has a wide database of foods available for either scanning a barcode or searching by name. I find the calorie count accurate, however for the macros tracker section it seems to be off calculation sometimes. Overall it's a decent app and is available for free if you stick with the basic version.

This app is super helpful for tracking my calorie intake. It's made me more aware of my portion sizes and snacking off my kid's plates. One odd blip: last weekend I hiked for 7 1/2 hours up and down a mountain. My calories for the day ended up being negative 2,200+. The app doesn't know how to show that day's info. It just skips that day, saying I haven't done 7 straight days. Weird, but I still really like the app.

This app is pretty good, especially if you get the premium version. However, there is some things that need to be fixed: 1) I'm having trouble loading the group I'm in and the app even crashed at one point. 2) I want to be able to add/see the type of fat being tracked, not just fat and saturated fat. 3) I'd like to see the vitamins able to be listed for each food. The biggest problem I'm having though, is loading the posts on my group's page. Sometimes it'll even say that I'm not in it but I am.

This app is awesome, and it's really helped me shed some pounds without too much trouble. If you struggle with dieting, tracking your calories makes it way simpler. I just wish the food database was a bit better, and it'd be cool if the dashboard showed your weight/fat% instead of just "recorded" when you log it.

Great easy to use app. Strong db of foods to choose from. Barcode feature works great on pricessed foods, plenty of options for building your own recipes and the app analyzes them for calories, protein, etc. i wish there was better analysis tools, though; would love to see what percentage of calories are coming from animal products, for example.

It's about the same as my fitness pal, but since I paid for the membership i get "insights" that let me see things like, "on days you drink coffee you eat less calories" ... which is useless because i literally drink coffee every single day. Also, as with any calorie tracker, tons of foods are just slightly inaccurate. Like a can of white claw is 100 calories, 2 carbs... but this says 110 calories and no carbs... aggravating! I would not have paid for the year and i known.

Few things to improve. Bug-consecutive day count is broken. For the premium options allow users to open the feature and explore it. You can still prevent logging in it until they buy. I am interested but hesitant to buy pro without actually seeing the features (what does glucose tracking do?). During the questionnaire you can say measurements or BF% or important to you but you can't log either of these metrics. I recommend adding these where you enter your weight.

Used to use this app way back when, decided to give it another go. Went ahead and sprung for the premium to be able to customize some goals. Really wish I could drop the number of meals lower than 4. I don't eat 4 times a day and I don't need an app to tell me to eat 4 times a day. Otherwise seems like a solid investment and will be my go to app for tracking.

The free version is excellent. I love the food tracking features including the food lookup abilities. I also love the progress graphs for your weight over time-it helps with motivation. I just don't like how frequently it bugs you to upgrade to premium. I have upgraded in the past and haven't found the added features very useful, but maybe that is just me. I wish they had a "don't ask me again" button instead of asking you to upgrade each time you sign in.

It's a great alternative to MyFitnessPal. It scans UPC codes, which is convenient. It also had the option to take photos of food, but it doesn't work well, and I would recommend they remove it to save on resources. The app freezes when certain things are clicked - even the link to get the premium version freezes and I need to force close the app. Otherwise, I felt envoy using the app, and no longer use MyFitnessPal (which is insanely expensive, and its free version is limited).

I am using the free version of the app. I like it so far. I enjoy the ease of using the app. I like the icons they show for the food, it gives you a nice visual besides numbers and words. I like the badges they give you, it is a nice touch. I also like checking in on the social feature. What i don't like is when you start a new day it takes a while for the date to switch over. i also don't like that their exercise calories spent are different than the planet fitness machines. i have to tweak the

I've never had much dedication to weight loss diet. Now, for health reasons I have more resolve, This app has been very easy to track my progress. I m using the free app, which is working just fine. But may join the paid app, which is only 2.99 a month, as I can track my water, and the exercise I am adding. I've tried to be very honest about the food, and have lost about 10lbs in a little over a month. 30 more to go!

The app is great, easy to use and very flexible for different ways of eating and tracking.I would give it five stars if my Lose It! scale would sync with my Android phone (Android and the scale not compatible). I also don't always trust the nutritional information that pops up when I search for a food. Many foods are accurate but some are way off. Otherwise it's my favorite tracking app of the ones I tried. I do like it better than MyFitnessPal mostly because Lose It! lets you track net carbs.

I've used this app over the course of 3 years. When I was on point logging everything, I lost upwards of 45 lbs in a year. I really enjoy the personalization and seeing how my timeline changes based on MY choices. It is a little User unfriendly, finding the right link to change goals and desired total calories is a little difficult but I spent 30 mins clicking on every thing and found what I was looking for. I went premium! It's worth it!

Great app, but...I wish the apps updated the articles they shared. I've had the same 2 forever. Also, I used to have an article at the top that I had to read as a check-off along with logging my meals BUT now I don't. I like the articles. I wish it could let you also make custom measurements, manually track certain nutrients like potassium, and have a place you can import pictures. Otherwise, I love the app and it is easy to use.

Overall, I love this app. I have lost over 7 pounds so far. The app has opened my eyes to portion control, how many calories I actually was consuming. The negatives There is no way for me to save a recipe for later use. I wish I could create my own weekly meal plan with the recipes in the app. AND there is no way to save all the nutritional info in "my recipes" without having to write them down and enter each ingredient. Recipes are not alphabetical.

Great app for calorie counting, the social side of it sucks hard though. The janky message system seems to have been made even worse than it already was by the new unecessary "feed" tab they added. You get permanent notifications (that you can't clear out) for messages that you can't view. Just stick to the calorie counting, but don't bother posting in the groups, it's not worth the headache.

It's a really great app! I've been logging since 10/12/19. It's user friendly and accurate but I knocked a star off for it seeming so...dated. The social areas are just long running threads and it's difficult to add friends. I wish it had better details on your exercises and options. I paid for the year but not sure if I will renew it after that.

So far, the app is great! I'm using the free version and it's doing the things I need. I do hate that a basic function such as water tracking requires paid subscription, but it's very inexpensive so maybe I'll go for it eventually...they've gotta make money somehow. Also, very limited accessories that can be connected and synced to the app, this is the biggest gripefrom me. Overall, it's been good 👍🏽

Great ap, using free version. Not too pushy with trying to get you to upgrade. Any questions I've had I've found answers by googling. Use daily to track food intake. Can see myself buying upgrade eventually; they frequently offer deals to do so. Losing fat isn't rocket science; just calorie deficit. Eyeballing and assuming you are eating healthy isn't accurate. This app is helpful to keep track. Be honest and consistent in recordkeeping and you'll get results.

I've been planning to rate this five stars but haven't taken the time to rate it untill now. But by now, when I finally am takeing the time to rate I've been disappointed. I still love the app it is very helpful in tracking calories and nutrients to get me the results I want. The reason I give it four stars is that, for some reason the app has become very slow. I don't think the problem is my phone because lose it is the only thing affected. It often freezes up and is "not responding".

I used this to lose 55 pounds. I ended up doing what I found to be called a Whole Food, Plant Based diet with some additional exercise. You can do it too. The app is good, but could stand to make tracking easier. I don't miss doing it every day. I'd also have been willing to pay to help support the development, but the plans are too expensive. I'd be okay with a cheaper tier, if only to give some deserved portions of my Google Rewards balance which I have to spend on Google Play anyway.

So far this has been my favorite tracking app, for both nutrition & exercise. The ease & swiftness of barcode scanning, picture taking auto fill nutrients, etc. are such a time saver & make this a genuine on-the-go app. I do; however, have two complaints, hence 4/5. I wish there was a skip meal button for intermittent fasting. I know, simply "don't log it, " but that leaves it looking incomplete. Also, maybe some nutrition guidelines/info for things like keto when it comes to tracking/meals.

i really love it, it helped me lose my baby weight 3 years ago, but there are a few little things that would really make it better. first only allow users to add food to the public database if the total calories and the breakdown (carb/fat/protein) matches. i hate using a food i found by searching then there's a gap on my micros tracker because there were unassigned calories. also I'd love a pregnant/breastfeeding option in the female health profile.

The barcode scanner used to NEVER work and or it would take an obscene amount of time to focus and read it. After the recent updates the barcode scanner scans all my items in seconds instead of minutes. It tracks exercise and the big three nutrients and gives an estimation of your TDEE and how long until you hit your goal weight. It only loses a star because it's still inconvenient if you're home cooking using ingredients and have to put everything in one by one manually every time.

Two things I didn't like about this app; 1, I have a full time job that alternates between reception and physical labor and I work out 6 days a week, I have no idea what activity level I fall under. 2, according to this app, losing weight at the rate I already have been would be extremely difficut and inadvisable.
