It was pretty smooth and easy to use, so I might try again.

I am a 55+ male and used Lose It! to lose 30 lbs in 5 months spanning the holidays. It is very good and easy to use, The paid version integrates my fitness watch. The food database, barcode scanner, and ability to store recipes makes tracking calories easy. With a few enhancements, it could be great! The food database allows the user to enter a food item by only one type of measurement: count (e.g. number of pretzels), volume (e.g. cup, teaspoon), or weight (e.g. grams, ounces). The nutritional information is often provided for at least two of these types. Further, when searching the database, it is unclear which of these entries will be useful for the measurement you have. The other issue is the unfortunate emphasis on "net calories", those consumed vs burned. When looking at your personal history, it is difficult to distinguish between a day on which you simply sat around and starved yourself versus a day in which you took a long hike and ate a hearty meal. The latter contributes more to overall health and should be more easily displayed and tracked. The app also provides the ability to look at your food history to see where you get your calories and the balance of your nutrition. These features are interesting and informational, but not as critical as the shortcomings expressed above.

Great app. Looks great, is fast and efficient, scanner works really well. It has a comprehensive index of products and allows you to customize entries that might not match due to a change. I don't like that you can't log food for tomorrow unless you pay; most other free apps have this. May not track as much as other apps, nutrient-wise.

For the most part, this app works great. My only issue is that when you create a recipe, you should be able to alter it each time you add it to a meal. For example, if i have a sausage, egg, and cheese muffin every morning for breakfast, i should be able to add it to the meal and go in and edit whether i had one sausage patty or two, one egg or two, etc. Also, i noticed that when you straight up edit the original recipe, the calories do not change accordingly. Five stars if they fix these.

I have only used this for 2 days. I will adjust my review after the month of May. But at this time, I find it so easy to find foods and brands with ease. Logging food is so easy. I also have connected the app with my Fitbit. Again no problems there. I want to look more into the whole program. I decided to go premium due to the low cost of this choice. I plan is comparing how I do with my food choices and watching calories.

I like this for calorie tracking, it's nice to have a visual on my intake. It remembers foods I eat and it congratulated me when I hit milestones like losing ten pounds, for example. I don't like how it asks me why I've "come back" when I haven't used it in a while. I did use it almost daily, but now I only use it for calorie info and to look at how far I've come on my weight loss plan. Being grilled just because I need to pop in is super annoying.

Been using free version for about one week. Like the UI, extensive data base, graphs. Am considering up grading to premium, but I have encountered some inaccuracies in the calorie counts - one as much as 50 cal difference. All have been under calculated. Wonder if all the other macro would be off as well? These errors were also noted as "verified" . If the database is not trustworthy there's no point in paying for premium which is a shame because I really am interested in the extra features.

I've used the free version of this app off and on for years. I'm now seriously working on some health issues by changing my diet. I need to be able to input homemade recipes in order to get correct calories, sodium, protein, etc. The ads didn't bother me. I'm trying Premium for one year to see if it's worth it; if it makes entering recipe content easier.

Updated 03/2022. I'm disappointed I paid for premium. It has more insights, which is great, and its easier to log meals. But the challenges are gone and its really hard to find new groups. Those were helping keep me motivated last time..... I love the extra features with premium. The insights page is helpful and the group's, forums and challenges keep you motivated. The app itself occasionally freezes, not more than once a week and I use the app daily.

Really happy with this one. I bought it, which makes all the difference. WHAT I LIKE: I can create recipes fairly easily, it remembers anything that I've used before, I can scan the barcode of a new food, and I can add foods from previous meals. WHAT I DON'T LIKE: It saves any food that anybody enters. This means that you have multiple entries for the same food because somebody didn't scan it, with different spellings, and with a completely useless 1 serving for the amount.

I got the pro version for a year and the app works great for what it's meant. I'd love to see under the "Health" option, along with the water/step/weight tracker, a multivitamin tracker. Just as an additional option, such as the water goal, to be able to keep track of everything in one place. The look of the app (visual, not layout) could use a little work to be more visually appealing as well, but the way it looks now isn't an issue as the features make up for it.

I really like this app. It has helped me lose about 15 pounds in about two months just by being a great food journal. The search capability is great and, if you're super small, you can input your own food items work nutrition information. I only gave it for stars because it keeps asking me to upgrade and I feel like there should be a snooze button on that advertisement for a week after someone clicks "no thanks".

I'm kidding, I still am. Though documenting my food intake was incredibly helpful as was the calorie goal. And I'm starting to see weight loss on the scales. The exercise input is good to have too. All the integration of other apps is very welcome as is the group input on food items. Would like to see more verified nutrition food items but pretty satisfied overall

This app it's great for tracking calories, macronutrients, and has a great food tracker. You could just scan any barcode and find exactly what you need. One thing that I would like to see is the ability to track intermittent fasting. Hey support, do you think you could add that?? Otherwise, it's a great all-around app for tracking all of your meals!

I've been using the free version for 593 days. I love it! For me, the biggest problem is that, when I enter a new food, there can be many entries of the same food (brand, serving size, etc) that all list different calories. Even if I switch it to "verified". Once I find the one that matches the calories on the box or package, I can edit it, enter it to my meal and it will then show up in "my foods". But I'd like to delete the incorrect ones that make the list so long.

I've been using this app for a few years on and off and I definitely prefer it's interface to all other weight loss apps. It's easy to use and fairly simple but provides a good amount of information. I do wish I could set short term goals as well as long term ones, for example, losing 5lbs this month. My biggest complaint is that it seems like a LOT of calorie information is wrong and when you're doing only 20g of carbs a day a few here and there really messes me up. I hate having to recheck.

Great, easy to use app with a very intuitive interface. Extensive food database. Quick and easy to input food. Many daily goal options: tracks macros, micros, fiber, water, etc. However, the program/plan portion is slightly cumbersome. It would be nice to have the option of manually settting calorie goals without setting up a program based on weight/height. I don't need the app to calculate this for me.

helps keep me on track with losing a few pounds and counting calories. I wish that I could get the premium can't really afford it. I love that it has pretty much every food and that you can also scan. Scanning make things a lot easier. The only thing is that when I put in my exercises sometimes I kind of have to make things up and it's not really specific. or compared to my other app the calories that I burned for the time that I worked out is off. But overall this is an amazing app.

The new addition of intermittent fasting is a nice addition to what I was already practicing. However, there needs to be more ability to customize the times. I don't always input as I eat, so to modify when a fast begins or ends instead of the 2 options of now or scheduled should be updated. Would like to see more exercise options be added as well. Otherwise great app, I've lost 30 lbs and maintaining or continuing to lose at a less aggressive pace is made easier!

Great software. Lose It helped me get my calories under control and I lost 36 pounds and feel so much better. My BMI is now normal and I look and feel great. The UI is intuitive and clean and the free features are good. They have a great food database and the search and auto complete are decent. Also they let you scan barcodes, which is very helpful. Regular ads are not obtrusive. Lows- premium feature ads are annoying. Also their maintaining weight recommendations are too high.

I've been logging for over 230 days and I've definitely lost weight in part to the app (around 20 lbs). It's great to be able to see a number and get a more realistic idea of the energy count of the food I eat. App is easy to use compared to similar apps. A bummer is there are features on the iversion that aren't available on android, like the ability to copy one of your own recipes and the constant updates are nice, but sometimes they break the app and take up a lot of space

I've had this app for years. I even paid for it way back when and even though I'm not tracking my food as much, I'm still using it to track my weight. And, I could have sworn, there used to be a little + symbol in the bottom middle when first opening the app that no longer exists. This button opened up the menu to log things including weight. But now I have to scroll til I find the weight spot which is easy to miss while scrolling amongst everything else. Please add this button back!

I was very impressed my this app at first. The layout is good and practical and the database is nicely fleshed out. I started to get a bit unhappy when I found that barcode scanning was behind the paywall, which is unusual for this sort of app from my experience. Over time such annoyances have added up. I had a lot more to say but hit the limit. Put more succinctly, it's a great app that's dragged down badly by its choices in regard to monetization. Still usable, but irritating.

I like the app but I'm not paying $40 just to have a barcode scanner and the ability to connect my Fitbit. I can do those for free on MFP. It is on sale right now but I can't justify paying $20 for something I get free on another app. If you have to pay for those options then you should offer a free trial. That way we make sure we like it before committing.

the app itself is pretty good, its just all the prompts to sign up for the lifetime version! I got a deal and paid $20 for a year not bad because I do believe in paying for apps that you actually like. but even though I paid for the year it's still prompts me all the time to pay $60 for a lifetime. I feel like since I already paid for the app for a year you should not be spamming me to pay even more money after I already paid

Very limited options, the maximum weight loss goal I could select was 2 pounds per week and the daily calorie estimates for me were too high. A lot of features are locked behind a paywall and the app is a bit pushy trying to get me to sign up for Premium. also worth noting that when I declined the paid service, they offered me a 25% discount to change my mind. When I declined that they offered me a 50% discount. Make of that what you will.

Okay, full disclosure. I like this app. I have used it for several years now and everytime I am consistent, I lose weight. the app does what it is supposed to do. I even started a subscription for some other basic stuff that wasn't necessary, but nice to have. But this latest update to the User Interface... They got rid of the middle "+" button that allowed for quick adds of weight or meals! Genuinely a dumb move in my opinion. The app still works, but they made it slightly more annoying.

Love the app, bought the lifetime subscription a while back and have had no complaints until recently. The last update made it impossible to scroll through all your nutrient trackers. You can only look at three at a time and have to switch them around in the settings before going back to the home page to see it. It's really important to me to be able to easily see all my nutrients and macros trackers and this update really ruins my experience. Very disappointed, would rate 5 stars otherwise.

I expect a paid app to fix update glitches faster. Portion sizes keep defaulting back to last used number after picking a time. Dark mode font colors are sometimes wrong. Too many "can't find" scans when the item is found on a second try. If the scanner is not finding something, then it should just try again immediately several times instead of just erroring out. Just delete blank database items. Only having a calorie number is not helpful. It's just bloat and a waste of time to sort through.

I've used this app for many years on an iPhone and recently switched over to Android. It's a great app. I rated it 3 stars because I really miss the multitude of widgets we had on IOS that seem to be lacking here on this system. Particularly the simple circle with calories/marcos left in the day. The one offered now is too big. Theirs also no way to get to the main page of the app from the widget. Very frustrating!! I would rate 5 stars if some of those widgets came back.

Updated/ new rating. I used to give it 1 star because they put the scanning feature behind the paywall. Then they finally gave it back. We'll now they put the amount of carb, protein, and fat behind the paywall. super annoying. Also PLEASE change back the layout. I liked to have an immediate scan button, now I have to hit the plus or pick a category before I can scan. Trust me. It doesnt make it cooler to make it more difficult.

update--recent updates have eliminated custom goals on the free version. and now I see the restaurant food search section has disappeared. keep taking things away. logging getting quirky. not improving. Overall good. Been using a long time now. Since the last update logging food has been buggy. When I try to add previous meals it will only add one or two items. Hopefully will fix in another update. Meets all my needs in tracking food, water, workouts and nutrition breakdown.

I love this app and have been using it decades. The last update moved the done logging check mark to the middle of the page underneath all the meals but before exercise and water and other goals. It used to be at the bottom of the screen so you could always find it easily just by scrolling all the way to the bottom. I never understand why apps make changes like this that actually make it less user friendly and absolutely no reason to move it from the bottom. Hope you change it back.

I love a lot of things about this app: the ability to scan barcodes, create recipes, repeat meals (especially handy when I enjoy leftovers) are really helpful. Beware of inaccurate food profiles. This app collects entries from a myriad of resources and accuracy varies widely. If you're careful, you're good.

It's an OK app. Lots of good stuff in it, but I feel like it's missing stuff too. I may be missing it somewhere, but for example, for being a paid app, you'd think they'd have a built in step counter, especially since its so focused on calorie intake and exercise. Seems like something that could easily be built in. This would would make it easier on the user to better track some activity.

I love and hate this app. The longer I use it, the slower it seems to get. It can take 30 seconds to load between actions sometimes and it's so frustrating because it happens multiple times a day. It was not at all like this when I first started using it in August. It can be buggy with logging food on the wrong day as well. Sometimes if I log food on a future day, it will add it to today's log instead. I'm paying for it for crying out loud, please make it run smoother!

I've used this app for years. I used to love it, but the latest version has made it virtually unusable. Quickly adding planned foods is now buggy with it clicking into the food details instead of adding it. The macros are per meal; no longer can you just scroll through each food to see macros. It's hard to find other micronutrient data now. It's slow and clunky. What happened??? I wrote to the company and they said it's fixed. What's fixed?

Mostly does what it's meant to, but it resets my calorie limit every week which is annoying. I have to manually change it back to the right one. Also i don't understand why i have to manually click "done logging" also, if I remember correctly, you used to be able to pay for a year's worth of premium at a time which netted you a discount and I don't think you can anymore. or at least I can't figure out how

With the update, you can no longer click an arrow at the top to see yesterday's results. Now you have to go to the dashboard, click on macronutrients, then hit daily at the top, then go back to the log. What a pain in the rear. Instead you have a calendar which shows where you stand pertaining to your calorie goal, which is what the dashboard is for. Put the arrow back, please!

Life-changing app thus far. For years, I did fine with exercise, but struggled with portion control & calorie counting. Now, I find it to be easy. So far I have lost 15 lbs in 3 months. Edit: I revise my review downwards, as food item search results are clogged with unhelpful items that arent verified, or calories are listed "per serving." This is BS. There are a limited number of meats, and they are either boiled, roasted, fried, etc. Calories should be known by gram and ounce, period.

What would make this app perfect is if I could change my calorie intake day by day and plan out my reverse dieting in app. Adding in 10 to 20 calories a day keeps me from gaining weight when I want to be eating more. edit 11/19/25 the app had gotten incredibly laggy. I've been using it for years and want to continue to use it, but with every update things get harder to use and slower. now I have adds to contend with in the middle of an entry.

I like this app and I've had it for a long time. I'm not a fan of the recent update though. Used to be able to see the individual nutrients in every item of food you consumed in a meal just by scrolling back and forth at the top your home feed. The update made the app worse and harder to navigate and keep track of everything. I try to get as much fiber and protein in my day as possible and being able to see what food items individually have that was a huge help. Fix please!

Not liking the new updates. Lose It has always been far superior to other similar apps, but I find the new interface on the tracking log clunky and way too busy. I am more interested in the calories I have consumed vs. calories I have left in my budget, and having to manually scroll up to see this information is frustrating. It would be one thing if you could personalize the layout to display what you find important, but are forced to view what the developers think is important.

I HATE THE NEW UPDATE!!! I've been using this app for nearly a decade and have been very satisfied. A few months ago everything changed... Food choices are limited. Navigation is harder. Viewing is crowded. I've purchased the Lifetime subscription, and now I'm considering dumping it entirely and using something else. This app went from being absolutely fantastic, to mediocre at best. And they want me to pay a "Lifetime membership" again.

Switched to this app over MFP awhile ago and overall have loved it in comparison. However, the most recent update has made the macro tracking on the app much more inconvenient than it used to be. I used to be able to see all my macro/micro goals and calories on the same tab and could EASILY swipe through them to see if they were met. Now i have to waste time switching back and forth between tabs to see all that info. Why update it just to make it MORE inconvenient?? Would be 5 stars otherwise...

this is a great app and really helps keep you accountable for what you put into your body. I've been using it since 2017 and have lost about 70 lbs overall with diet, exercise and this app. Last week I would have given it 5 stars but this latest update is terrible. I don't know what was broken before that they felt the need to "fix" it. it used to show your budget, total calories consumed and your net after exercise. it no longer has the "net" and looks totally different and not in a good way.

The only reason why i subscribed annually was to log my fats. sugar and protein on the log section of lose it. now it only allows to track 3 nutrients instead of more like before. it even says on the settings section of lose it that only the top 3 nutrients will be shown on the log header. how can I make it where it showed everything like before? its a major inconvenience to go to the dash board now and having to do so much just to see the nutrients where before they were at a glance on the log.

Premium still has ads, minimal reporting of nutrients like calcium. To explain: I paid for the lifetime and everything, but it still pops up questions about paying for more things. And, one is even "a gift", but when you look, they are all external paid-for programs with a 'gift'. It is all very distracting, and a premium lifetime user should have have to constantly dismiss these. Further, after diligently entering all my food, i cant get back how much calcium or iron consumed.

Disappointed in the Android version. The Health Connect integrations are not as complete as they are in IOS. I have blood pressure and hydration being logged to health connect and other apps read it. lose it is not updating the log. I recently switched to Android from iOS and I can say the iOS lose it app is less buggy and more complete. Im a lifetime premium member and have been using lose it since 2016.

A battery draining app! I really liked the UI on the app. It was simple and had all the food choices available. I went from a 100% battery to 32% in 20 minutes. I removed the app and have had stable battery since. I was only able to use it for 3 days before realizing it was the reason for my dying phone. fix the issue and I will return, please.

The advertising has become less aggressive, but the adverts still go on too long. I find it's actually quicker to click out of the app & go back in than watch the whole add, which surely defeats the object of the advertisers. If they made the ads shorter they would be less annoying & id probably watch them.

Update: I left the app for some time and when I came back I assumed the many recipes and foods I added would still be there. I was wrong! Everything I painstakingly added is GONE. What's the point of having an account then if your information doesn't stay? Original: I have been using this app for slightly over a week and I absolutely love it, especially compared to the S Health app that came installed on my phone. The barcode scanner has been incredibly accurate in my experience.

I've had to edit my original review. "This app is easy to use and add food and exercise. I only wish I could sync it to my sports tracker account. So happy they put the reps back." A lot of this is true and it helped me to get to where I wanted to be. Here is where it has lost stars: They have moved some of the previously free features behind the pay wall. I can no longer scan food in. The heat maps are a thing of the past. It doesn't allow me to sync my fitbit. Disappointing.

I really, really wish counting Added Sugars was available in the way that counting Fiber, Saturated Fats, or Net Carbs are. Unfortunately, it is not even an option when filling in the nutrition info for foods. If the American Heart Association and others recommend a certain maximum amount of added sugars, it would make sense to add that sort of information in the same way they do other micros for tracking. Otherwise, I enjoy the app. I have not had any problems with it linking to Fitbit's app.

I liked the recipe feature in the paid version and easy to use interface. The database contains lots of inaccurate items making it difficult to select. Many entries don't contain nutrient data. There is a "verified only" switch but drastically limits the choices. It's a good value IMO if you don't mind wading through the data.

I've used and loved this app on/off for years and it helps me stay on track when I'm wanting to watch my intake. I've gone back and forth bw the free & premium version, depending on my situation and needs at the time. But CONS: • No way to mass delete old recipes. • It was a stupid idea to make the barcode scanner a part of premium when it has been a basic/free feature included for years. • The pressure / pop-ups to switch to premium has increased & gotten a lot more annoying.

I needed an app to count my calories, and tried a few different ones. This one had a good deal for a year-long susceptibility, so I ended up getting it, but there are a couple of issues: 1. I already paid for a year-long subscription. Stop advertising the life-long subscription every time I open the app. It's annoying. You already have my money, so stop asking. 2. The system for adding foods isn't as good as some other ones out there. Would be nice to auto-add estimated nutrition for new foods

As others have mentioned, the "Done logging" toggle stopped functioning properly. I have to close the app and then select "Done logging" for it to work, and in doing so it pops up a full screen ad that didn't used to exist prior. So the issue seems like the app is just failing to open the ad when selecting "Done logging", and thus not completing the toggle process. My advice to solve this, get rid of this unnecessary intrusive ad spam.

I love the update, adding food is easy. This app allows me to keep track of my calories, water intake and macros also. **Note that I have the premium account. However, keeping track of my weight loss is a little discouraging. The graph shows my weight loss but the numeric proof is not calculating correctly.

I purchased the Pro version to get the exercises. However, the pictures are so blurry you cannot tell what you should be doing. I emailed them but no response. Also bar code scanner is slow to come up. Calorie tracking is good & is on the free version. Overall, for me, it's not worth what I paid for the upgrade.

I used LoseIt for 2 years and lost ~45 lbs! This was a great app to build the habit of tracking - easy to use, simple interface, large database of foods. I hoped it would update with new features over time that allow you to get more out of your data but I never saw much improvement. Now that I'm further along in my fitness journey, I need more data insights and integrations. I switched to MacroFactor and love how much more advanced it is. Still thankful to LoseIt for helping me get started!

This app used to be one of the top weight loss apps you can get. It had a lot of features that were free and they didn't bug you to upgrade to the lifetime all the time. Recently decided to get back on to lose some weight but the app bare Bones does just the bare minimum of keeping track of calories. Also the database need some work, if you look something up you'll have to dig through the many many entries to verify you're logging the correct item. Also would be nice to have recipes.

I used this app for several years, and took a few months off it because I was sick. It was great, and even free it had everything I need. But I've come back to it and found that the barcode scanner is seemingly pay-walled? Every time I try to use the scanner it just prompts me to pay for premium. Maybe this is a bug that's being caused by the sale but I can't use the barcode scanner for free and honestly that's a dealbreaker. Almost every other fitness tracker app I know let's you do it for free

Needs custom macros .... overall I like this app, but I do not like that their "custom" plans cannot be customized. The high protein setting is not high enough for the macros I'm trying to hit. It is 90g and I need 130g setting. Looks like they used to have the ability to customize and removed it for some reason. Please bring this feature back.

Look, I really like this app. Unfortunately ever other thing I look at in it is something telling me how I should upgrade. Please give me the ability to remove the adds. I'm NEVER going to pay for an app annually. If you want to charge to unlock for features that are basically addition, subtraction, and sorting, fine, I'll pay once for that, but please stop accosting me ever time I open your app or add a meal to upgrade. It's really annoying. Other than that it's really a great app.

The layout and general setup were ok but a lot of the nutritional info was incorrect, even when scanning barcodes. Also a lot of items forced you to log in 'servings' instead of allowing lbs, fl oz, grams, etc. ...So if I'm logging a potato for example, it makes me say how many servings I had... I don't know what a serving of potato is, but I know the weight of the potato I had so it would be a lot easier to just be able to enter that.

I wish there was some way to export the data in to excel (.csv) All the foods, times, and calories, carbs, fats, data are locked in the app. More convenient then a note book I guess. 48 days straight day logging and now I would like to examine the data but I can't. If I could export all the trapped data I would purchase the app. I guess I'll keep using it while looking for an app that has useful reporting... I can do the basic math myself.

Okay for calorie counting, less so for nutrition. Will not let me set my own daily calorie ceiling or date at which I want to reach my goal weight by, just weekly loss rate which isn't important to me. No point in adding vitamins since it doesn't log minerals. The macronutrients it does track are very basic, even if you pay to see more. Often the barcode scanner brings up information on the wrong product or shows no results at all. App is locked to dark mode if your phone is using it. Ugh.

Eh everything costs money now. Not just this app but all of them. I just want a simple nutrition tracker, not just calories. I'm trying to pay attention to protien intake and fiber as well. But you can't see that on any of the apps I've found without paying. That's okay, it's not that hard to keep track yourself just takes extra work. I'm not paying for that.

This app isn't much different than many of the other apps I've tried. Lost 10 lbs with my fitness pal, but I set this app to lose 1 lb a week and in four weeks I've only lost 1 lb! I didn't even include my exercise which I do 5-6 days a week, so I've been under their recommended calorie count and still haven't lost the 1 lb a week they told me I would. I also don't like the subscription model. My fitness pal included many of the things this app wants to charge me for.

Pretty good... but one big flaw (for Android). Me and my wife eat a lot of the same meals, but there is no way to share the details once all of the entries are made on my phone or hers. So, we both have to enter all of the same details. It would be much easier if she could just send and I import into whatever meal I need. Also, it doesn't I directly integrate with Samsung Health. With all of the Samsung devices, I would expect that integration to be there. Other than that.. app is pretty good.

Good app... but only if you pay for the premium package. I did the 7-Day free trial and really enjoyed the scanning option, but wasn't sure I liked it enough to pay for it, so I switched to the free version. Because the Fitbit connection is free on most of the other ones I've tried, like MyFitnessPal, I did not anticipate that if I didn't pay for the premium, that I wouldn't even be able to track my steps at all. Really disappointed that I can't even add them manually. Kind of useless now.

Quite a good app but the ads are worse and worse all the time. I literally have to close the app to make the ad go away, and then open it up again....several times just to input a single day's data. PLEASE DO NOT TELL PEOPLE TO PAY YOU $ TO REMOVE SOMETHING YOU SHOULD NOT HAVE PUT THERE IN THE FIRST PLACE. It's like punching people and then saying, "No problem. Just pay us to stop."

Used for awhile And it was great. I stopped using and recently began again, however the app's exercise options are going wonky and I can't figure out why. I try to input my walks on the treadmill, using my own calories lost and duration, and its giving me crazy calories lost numbers- in the thousands! It won't save my input. What's up with that? I can't use the app now because it keeps logging 2,700 calories lost for a 30 minute walk lol. Once that gets fixed I'll get back on it .

This is the best weight loss app I've used so far. But I have one complaint. I've been using Lose It for literal years (almost a decade). I use the basic version and not the paid version. I went to scan a food item today and apparently I can only do that if I pay now?? What the hell, man? That used to be one of the best features of Lose It, & anyone could use that feature, and now you're telling me you've yanked it from basic users & only premium users can scan food now? What a money grab.

Okay 3 stars. This app is really great for keeping track of what you're eating! its got the scanner that recognizes real store barcodes and can really help you to understand and control portion sizes. However, this app is seriously lacking in the community factor. Support groups/friends are one of the biggest parts to a successful weight loss. App needs some major updating to the social aspects such as finding groups/people and being able to smoothly connect. (and only email notifications 👎)

Not a bad app but not the best. What I really need is to see how many macros I actually have left without having to click on them individually. That would be extremely helpful. I see what they did with displaying them when you click in them but I'd love to he able to open my log and see how much more of each that I need to eat. The database has almost everything in it that you can imagine. I am a premium user because on black Friday it was on sale for $20 for a year. Too cheap to pass on. I'll put up with my small gripes but aside from that it's a good app.

I have used this app for several years. I recently stopped paying for premium. I like the app but it's too glitchy to spend money on it. Too often it stops syncing with Google Fit. Food I enter into My Foods goes away. Sometimes weight gets measured in hours. My exercises are not in the app. I think developers have a right to charge for their product but I don't want to pay for a product that is not dependable. I don't really want to spend money just to be frustrated.

I want to like this app. Most of the time food is easy to enter and the barcode scanner is the best. My problem is that the choice of measurement options doesn't work for me. My food scale has grams and ounces. Lost It doesn't always give those options. I don't want to convert grams to cups when I enter a food. If that doesn't bother you, I recommend you give this a try. It's unusable for me.

TL;DR: To lose weight, you need to track more than just calories, and you need to double check the macros on the app. This app is good for the most part, but you NEED to double check anything you didn't log yourself. Because everything on the app is added by normal people, it can be very inaccurate, even if it has the "Verified Check". For example, when logging Jersey Mike's subs, even the "Verified" sandwiches were sometimes off by almost 100 calories, or by 11g of protein. Just ridiculous.

A lot of the "verified" items are wrong. The calorie and nutrition numbers are lower on the "verified" items than on the actual packaging or company websites. Why even have "verified" items? I see no option on the items to report they are wrong either. I wish there was a drink category and dessert category rather than just breakfast, lunch, dinner, snack.

For years, this app has done a fine job helping me track foods I eat throughout the day... but lately it's been nagging (and I mean NAGGING!) me, sometimes multiple times a day, to upgrade to the premium level. It's to the point that I almost no longer want to use it, and may stop recommending it to others. Ease off the nagging, Lose It!

Lose It app was perfect 4 years ago except for the thousands of "foods" that people entered incorrectly. Some of the "foods" were just made up! calories are way off. You can stop that problem by entering everything yourself. However, Lose It app brags about the "huge number" of foods in its database. Most are duplicates or are made up/incorrect. Manufacturers update their recipes and packaging, the "foods" on the Lose It app are never updated. This is why they have so many foods listed.

I love a lot about this app. I'm switching my strategy now to focus more on macros. I wanted to customize my goals. The process to set customized macros is needlessly confusing. For some reason, it isn't in the same location as "Goals". Instead you have to go to your log and click on the specific macro, click settings, and then set it. Why? Just, why? Why make it more difficult and unintuitive. I was half a second away from switching apps, but luckily I figured it out.

update 5/4/2025: my recipe builder is not working? please fix? The premium version of the app is great. The free version sucks. Lots of features. I'm a visual person, so I'm obsessed with the food graphics. You can edit the nutrition information of a food that's already saved if it's incorrect. You can create your own recipes. Some things take some getting used to and can be hard to find at times, but it's a nice app overall. I like it more than MyFitnessPal, though I did like their charts.

Just a few years ago everything or just about everything on this app was free to use. It sucks you have to pay just to log in your water intake or other very basic things. The free version isn't really worth using when on any other diet app you get so much more free. Hopefully they'll make some changes but until then I'm going elsewhere. Best of luck to everyone out there trying to make healthy changes.

Be aware that even if you buy a lifetime membership they still charge you for access to a lot of things. They offered me a discounted price almost immediately to go from an annual subscription to lifetime access. I wasn't familiar with the app yet. I went for the lifetime membership and found that this is for BASIC access to the app. If you want to look at any of their educational materials, it's all extra. Be sure you're OK with paying for lots of other things before you commit to it.

Paid $20 for the year & expected more for premium upgrade. A bit frustrating with certain features. 1) did not adjust intermittent fasting for daylight savings time. Had to change entry to reflect 1 hour earlier as a start time. Not very intuitive. 2) can't figure out how to add previous meals for quick entry. Tried and it will add the meal but the nutrient info including calories doesn't transfer over. 3) no Samsung/ galaxy watch sync option.

I've used this for years but lately the ads have been after every. single. thing. i. do. Log one thing and it's an ad immediately. Close the day out and I don't even get to see the little counter for the day finished for more than half a second before an ad shows up. I scan one thing and it's an ad. This is insufferable. I'm going to go back to pen and paper if this continues to be insufferable.

9/6/24 I don't think that it deserves a different rating. I just wanted to know why when I go to log my meals for the day, it now shows me a (-436) in the exercise spot before I even do any exercises. It throws me off with my calorie budget making it seem like I have more calories to use and I don't. This started Wednesday, 9/4/24. Did you all do something else to the app? HELP!

The app is constantly "forgetting" food items previously used. Especially if there are multiple versions, eg. Unsweetened soy milk and original soy milk. It'll forget one or the other completely, as if it thinks I wouldn't have possession of both types at once. I guess the name of the game is to make the app suck at its main job unless you pay for premium.

Complex system making logging new things & figuring out the stats challenging. I don't really get anything for the insights, it misses a lot. I'm apparently super healthy & eating perfectly(not really). I can't tell if one day logged is a healthy day or not. I bought the subscription for insights and I don't feel like I got what I wanted out of it - advice. I don't feel the subscription was worth purchasing.

I was recommended to use this app by my dietician and within the first month I've lost a little over 15lbs. I like how easy it is to log food but I've found a majority of the time if it's 'premade' food it's inaccurate. It's time consuming to edit everything to make sure it's correct. I also wish I could pair my Samsung watch to the app since it tracks my steps, heartrate and sleep already, especially since I bought the premium.

The free trial days, it was great. It synced my calories burned with my Garmin. It didn't sync my water amount, but that was okay. As soon as I paid for a subscription it no longer pulls calories burned from my Garmin I had to log out of the app and back in it finally read my Garmin but I lost the last 6 days of food entry. You have to back up with a screenshot of everything you entered so you can reenter it, which sadly I didn't know, so I just last several days of entries very frustrating

It does what it says but it's not perfect. I understand that they want to make money through subscriptions but why not let me pay for a lifetime license? This seems unreasonable for an app that acts like a glorified calorie calculator. The advertising is also quite intrusive. The "limited time" sale is perpetually there, don't be tricked... That's kind of sketchy

It's a good motivator, but I have found that the nutrition information is often wrong when the app enters it into your log, even when scanning the barcode from the food item itself, so I have to create most of the foods I enter. That makes me concerned about the validity of the nutrition info for all the foods already stored in the app.

Not worth it anymore I have used this app on and off for years and always used the free version. I came back to it a few days ago and saw that I could input my food as usual but can no longer use the barcode scanner unless I am a premium member! I refuse to pay for any calorie counter app as I can do it myself. I chose to use another app. This app has a premium version as well,but as of now , can input food and use the scanner for free. It also has a lot of other features that are pretty cool

This app is good for monitoring portions and what not, but I'm very disappointed with what I was greeted to when I opened the app. First of all, you need to be 18 to have a profile on this app... Seriously? You need to be 18 to have a dietary plan and lose weight? Like you can't do anything without being an adult. On top of that, they immediately put you on the paid option for the app and you have to manually chose the free version. Super scummy.

For calorie counting on its own, this is a great app. It is extremely intuitive and the systems it uses to recall data make it very easy to enter after you've been using it for a while. That said, it has gotten extremely aggressive in its sales pitch. Between the launch pop-ups, the "flash" sale ticker that has been going for months, and the full half of the data entry dashboard taken up to show upgrade features, the self-promotion is easily as intrusive as any ad-supported app I've used.

I used LoseIt for years, and have finally made the decision to move to another app as I have found the macros (and sometimes calories) on most foods here are not accurate. If you're fine with double checking everything or if you have a pretty basic diet, the app is great. I love the interface much more than other apps, all the different options you get with premium, and the groups. I just do not have the time to manually enter or correct every entry as I eat a large variety of foods.

I've used this app for years. My only issue is that "user shared" products are almost always wrong (when compared to the label), and there isn't always a "verified" version. Often times there is no info at all, except the calories (which are usually wrong, too) It sucks having to edit, or create from scratch, so many items. It would be nice if they'd stop making "share with users" the default. Most people won't stop to uncheck the box, or check that their entry is accurate.

I really liked this app and it definiatly was fun to track my food and lose some weight. After the recent update Oct 2018, it now comes up with option to enter number for servings or amount of items, instead of being able to scroll through numbers. I hate the new update, i don't want the number pad to come up every time i select a food item, i would rather scroll like it was before. why change something that was just fine like it was. I was thinking of getting the premuim version, but if it is going to be annoying having the keyboard pad come up every time, then forget that.

Maybe I'm missing something, but there doesn't seem to be a way to enter fractions of servings? e.g. if a chocolate bar is 56g and I eat half, I need to do mental math (56/2 = 28g) instead of just entering 0.5 like I can in other apps. This really slows things down and I can forget to enter foods while trying to figure out the correct portion size of others :( otherwise great app. Edit: some foods do have an "each" or "serving" option which makes this slightly easier, but that's not great.

Easy to use with an exercise section that deducts calories burned by a long list of activities, including weight lifting. Previous items and even entire meals from previous days can be added as a convenience. However. Lately the app has slowed down. I'll select a food item and nothing at all happens for maybe five seconds, then--finally--it appears in my log. These delays seems to have increased in direct proportion to appeals to upgrade to the premium version, which I don't need.

This app is fine. I switched to this from MyFitnessPal a few months ago on a friend's suggestion. I loved their "pattern" recognition where it would notice I was more or less likely to go over or under calories with certain foods. However, they moved this behind a paywall. They also used to have more statistics and carb goals but then they moved those behind a paywall too. Almost every reason I was using this over MFP has been moved behind a paywall, and while I'd be happy to watch some ads or something to unlock features for the month that's not an option. I'm moving back to MFP because, while I think this is a better app for paid users I am not one. Now the free version is just a tracker with less features than MFP. Good app, stable, great pay features but too hollow as a free tracker.

Every time I log in, it tells me I'll save a percentage on life time. "Save 50%" "Save 62%" etc etc. The money saved isn't anything. The price keeps getting higher and the percentage gets tweaked to make it seem like I'd be saving money. The offer sucks and it's annoying to see every time I log in. Other than that, it's a fine app. Kind of hard to navigate and make changes to plans and goals. But the tracking of goals is good.

IT's not a bad little app but it constantly bugs you to upgrade to the pay version.  A lot of home cooked items are not in the database.  For many items there are pages and pages of the same thing with a serving size of 1 but no mention of how big that serving size is.  You can edit a recipe but you can only add and remove items you cannot adjust the quantity/size.

I had this app two years ago and it seemed easier to do stuff on than now. I can't search for challenges because when I got search it doesn't work, the foods I look up have many different calories for the same food and servings. And it's even more push-y to have me upgrade to premium. I get having ads, but when the "ads" are just the app promoting it's paid portion it gets irritating. Also, please fix the search for challenges. I want to do more than just basic logging challenges and can't.

Used the app years ago & it was great. Seems to have been upgraded and is more complicated to use now. I wish it would allow me to completely delete all my past failures when I reset goals or restart a diet. No one wants to be reminded of a long history of diet failures!! I've switched to the keto diet so I'll just use their specific app.

I like this app so far. It's relatively basic without buying the add ons for more assistance in what foods are helping or holding you back. It asks in the set up if you are managing any health conditions while dieting, and doesn't ask anything further upon selecting yes. Seems silly and pointless. Not very personalized without buying a premium membership. Also, as a dancer, it would be nice to be able to log dance activities as more than general "dance" in exercise. Decent app overall.

I like the app. However it is not very accurate, and is frustrating at fimes because you can have four or five entries for the same food item, all using different measurements that add up to different amounts of calories. Also the excersize amounts seem off. My elliptical says one number and this app says something completely different.

Pros: food database is reasonable, bar code scanner also, price is not insane unlike MyFitnessPal. Cons: adding food and tracking your own recipes is not as easy as it should be. Many food entries in the database offer one single size "1 portion", very uninformative. Having a weight or volume to go with what that means would help, as would allowing you to specify the amount in your units of choice for any entry. Similar story for your own recipes. But, also, not sure what's better out there.

Technically 3.5 stars. It's a decent free app if all you want to do is count calories. Oftentimes the nutrition facts are incorrect, but I understand that not everyone is calculating the same. Plus packaging can sometimes be different depending on where you're located. I do miss the free barcode scanner, and wish there were more free options. How about a pay for what you need option? Lately every time I open the app, it opens to "Cooking up an update". It's getting really annoying.

I love the app especially because I can use it on my PC too! Much easier for me because I don't live on my phone like so many do. I'm losing weight and feel better already. I down voted it two stars because the food library is massive but I've found 90% of the nutrient numbers are wildly inaccurate!!! When you add a food it goes into the main library as well for others to use. The problem is people must be grabbing numbers out of thin air. Most nutrient numbers are absolutely laughable. DO NOT USE THE FOOD LIBRARY if your nutrient totals important to you. It's seriously THAT bad..... Now I take the actual food in hand and add it to my library using the nutrient figures on the package. I wish they would go through, verify and remove all of the inaccurate foods from the library.

The scanning of barcodes was slow and their database does not include grams for some foods when entering foods. How can an app even call itself a calorie counter and not have the grams for butter (Smart Balance actually) in their database? This is supposed to make it easier to track your calories. How is having to measure the butter for your toast out in teaspoons easier?  I also find the interface when entering food to be confusing. The designers did not take into account that when the text entry field is active, half of the screen is taken up with the keyboard and it covers up all of the options they show you from prior meals.  It also has the same perverse obsession that all of these apps with classifying food intake according to breakfast, lunch, and dinner. But I did not take any stars off for that since that seems to be a pandemic problem in all of the counters.   One star off for the clunky interface that does not focus on what they user actually wants to see and one off for a database that lacks important data.

I really enjoy this, makes keeping track of everything quite easy, and it does all the counting for you. However all of a sudden the fat carb and protien tracker wont show me how many grams I've had a day, have had the app for not even a full week yet and all my tracking of those are gone/hidden. If it's a premium feature I wasnt made aware upon downloading, and if it stays that way I'll saddly have to find a new app to track these things. Other than that it's very user friendly

Food database is extensive so it's easy to enter meals. Also easy to enter exercise (except walking) but the app defaults to increasing the calories you can consume if you enter exercise. This will lengthen the time needed to lose weight. You can only change that setting each day rather than changing it once for all days. I consider this a major flaw they should address.

After a recent update, the app runs extremely slow when entering foods. I type in the food name, and 40 to 50 seconds later it might pop up some results - sometimes it just freezes. Also, before the update, it would show recently logged items (that match my search) at the top... not anymore. Now, it takes much longer to log meals. Very frustrating. I've tried paring down a large amount of my saved foods, hoping that would speed it up, but it didn't.

Incredible food database plus add your own food and recipes, gentle reminders to keep logging if you forget, and so easy to use. I'm actually losing weight with this app. BUT...the most recent update messed up the way you enter foos portions and makes it much more difficult. Please bring back the old way of inputting portions! This update made me change my mind on the Premium upgrade....

Hi! I have used this for several years. It used to be more user-friendly when trying to change grams to ounces or pieces, etc. Used to do automatic conversion when changed, too. Not anymore. This version has a bug trying to save nutrition updates manually input, especially if the item was originally scanned by the barcode. Irritating when you have done all the math and it throws the changes out, keeping the original data. Please fix these issues. Thanks!

It's a great way to track calories, it's just that you have to make sure you are creating your own foods to ensure the calories are correct. My mistake was using the "search" bar and using the "verified" versions of the food. Big mistake. It turns out, those foods were underestimating the actual calorie count. Wasted days mis-calculating my foods and not losing weight.

latest update is TERRIBLE. now a large panel of redundant buttons pops up when you "add food" all those buttoms are already available on this page, but now I can only see two food choices. The food choices are now larger and even less compact than before which also limits what you can see. PLEASE make everything more compact so we can see more than one or two items on any given screen, and stop those buttons from popping up!

I switched from MyFitnessPal when they started charging for a significant number of features that had been part of the basic app. Now I've switched from this app to VirtualGym's Food app for the same reason. I understand the need for revenue, but being a diabetic, I only care about carbs. I don't care about all the other bells & whistles they removed, like bar code scanning. Removing nutrition info was the final straw. Sorry to leave an otherwise good system.

This used to be my go-to calorie tracking app due to its ease of use. However, with the newest updates, the free version has become almost completely unusable. Ads pop up every other time you try to add something to your meal diary, which frequently causes my phone to freeze. Many times, it takes a good 15-20 seconds for the app to load at all. This app used to be very quick to use, but now it is undated with ads and entries cannot be added quickly.

Hope the quick-record button disappearing on January 31st was a glitch. UPDATE 2/4/26. it was not, in fact, a glitch. The devs seem married to the idea that no one used the Quick-Add button and don't seem to be listening to feedback. Also as others have noted there are way more ads znd pop-ups even as a paid user. Glad my subscription was annual not lifetime, sorry for those who paid big bucks for a evolving experience.

I HATE the new update. I have a lifetime membership and just came back to lose it. The new update is so limiting. there use to be a button I could click on and I could toggle all nutrients/macros, now it just shows members benefits. I also use to be able show different macro/nutrients per meal, that option is gone. I'm so disappointed. not everyone just tracks protein fat and carbs. One of the reasons I choose this app is the freedom to customize what/how I wanted to track, now it's gone.

The newest update not only didn't fix the fact that it pulls up 2 of my 9 breakfast items, but moved the finish logging button to the middle of the page. Don't fix what isn't broken. But, for anyone else struggling with the missing breakfast items, just click add, then go to meals and add yesterday's breakfast. It still works that way. But please, stop with the changes, it's not improving anything.

Updated Review: Improvements have been made in the past year which I liked, but unfortunately something has been going on with this app for months now that makes it very difficult to use. It... is... so... sloooooow. Constantly freezing up with every input. Crashing. I've tried uninstalling and reinstalling. Clearing the cache. Nothing helps. It is so bad now that I want to cancel my subscription and move to another app. Unfortunate.

I really don't like the new interface where you can only select 6 macros to follow and it doesn't show you where those macros are coming from in your meals. Seems more like a downgrade than an upgrade. Now there is only a meal-level breakdown or macros, but not the components of the meal so it is now harder for me to figure out what is causing me to go over on something like sugar or saturated fat. I am reverting to an older version where we could see that info.

This app itself is very pretty and the food icons are cute, but the sheer number of incorrect food entries is unbelievable! Even the 'verified' entries are often incorrect. I also wish there was a way to filter entries with weight rather than volume. Too often I'll search for a food and find that serving size isn't grams, oz, or even cups but rather some like '1 Piece' or '1 Packet'.

I really dislike the latest update! Obviously being new, it will be difficult to read at first, but I really don't like how the nutrients show and the difference in how it looks when we search for foods. I think the worst part is only being able to track 3 nutrients at the top of the daily log. Some of us need to view way more than 3 like before. I get changes to the interface to stay with the times, but these weren't necessary. At least revert the change to seeing only 3, PLEASE.

Not sure WHY they removed all the different goals (calories, carbs, sugars, protein, fiber, etc) from the top of the page. You can no longer see how much of the 9 goal you have achieved over the course of the day. They removed the swipe feature for the 9 goals. Now you can only pick 3. The other 6 are blank in the settings. Very glad I did not purchase the lifetime subscription. I'll finish out the year subscription and find a more user friendly app!!

The latest update is too big, as in literally too big. Less information is seen on screen as they increased the size of each item. WHY? Saving doesn't actually save until you save more than once. Example: I search for the bread I always eat. I change the default 1 slice to 2 slices and hit Save. It doesn't save the 2 slices. It saves 1 slice. I have to repeat the quantity change and saving 2-3 times for the correct quantity to save. I have to do this for every entry I make!!!

The recent updates to layouts and options is terrible. I've used this app twice this year, both times buying premium. Not only did the nutrient drop down get removed (where you can see your nutrient's overall goal at the top and then each food's breakdown was below), but I still get ads to buy premium even tho I got a year's subscription. You used to get way more detail and options on how to view your nutrient data. The log layout is awful now.

New UI is awful. Bubble look with large padding. Search term does not delete when you add a new food when clicking the checkmark. I think they were trying to streamline adding foods you eat often, but I need to change the serving amounts and that doesn't pop up unless you click the line item. I end up having double entries and need to go back and delete items. Made it clunkier than it already was. I would think if you did a bulk checkmark it should verify amounts before it adds to your list.

The newest update is pretty bad. While it's easier to add food, the front display is awful. Previously, it was so easy to swipe between calories, Macro breakdown, and then you had the option to add a specific nutrient (I liked seeing protein). But now, it's complicated and completely not user friendly. Please change this back.

it's an okay app, missing a lot of key features. I'm rating it poorly because I'm constantly bombarded with ads asking if I want to buy the lifetime premium membership. it's always a "final call" (but happens sometimes daily for some reason). i already have the yearly membership. I really wish they would stop throwing a pop up to advertise it, it's quite annoying.

I use this for basic tracking and I don't need a lot of the additional features but I was tracking my weight and posting a photo for my weight loss Journey over the last year and a half and now all the photos I posted are gone. It says it is NOW a Premium feature, and I don't know if it would put back all the previous progress if I upgraded, but it's just not worth that much money for basic tracking. I'm so disappointed, and a heads-up warning would have been appreciated.

I used to love the app. Useful features and a clean UI. However, over the years, the ads for "premium" subscription have gotten more and more aggressive, at least 3+ popups before you can actually use the app. And now, developers have removed basic functionality and features (like scanning barcodes) that used to be free, and put them behind a subscription in a blatant cash grab. After 4 years, I will be uninstalling and moving to another app. I suggest you do the same.

I moved from MyFitnessPal to this app about a year ago when MyFitnessPal took away the free barcode scanner. Well, now Lose It! has gone the same way as MFP unfortunately. I'm actively searching for a better app. I'd understand it if they wanted to charge for new features, but pay walling old features is very frustrating. I used to be a big advocate for this app at work and with friends, but not anymore. Enjoy the fruits of your greed I guess. A one time paid version would be nice too.

As all the other reviews say, the new update is not good for selecting quantities easily. It wasn't very good before though, as it didn't have the proper scale. Anyways, the free version is becoming unusable thanks to self promotional ads for premium. I get it, you guys need money, but you're ruining your brand and you're losing the respect of free users. You need to change something, and spamming free users with premium ads is only going to push away users that might one day pay for it.

Doesn't work on my phone. Keeps crashing, or not open at all. I love the realistic goals it sets for you and tries to understand your goal more, but the app just won't work for my phone. Bummer. Also, for logging in foods, some of the barcodes lead to the wrong items, and when you're uploading your own entry, there's not an option to put in the barcode number. I'm going back to NyNetDiary.

Pretty decent calorie counting and weight management. There are a lot of features, the main issue I have is the massive amount of advertisement even with the Premium edition. I've tried to reach out to the developers, with no luck. You are sent through an AI chat that ends up disconnecting you anyways. If you get this app just use the free version.

The app has gotten worse. Everything is locked behind a subscription now. Furthermore, the app straight lies to you to get you to buy the subscription. IT DOES NOT LINK TO FITBIT. All it does is track steps and calorie goals, something Fitbit already does. It does not sync food, or calorie intake, like it claims to when you try to set it up. What a shame. I loved this app, but it's just turned into a scummy, overpriced scam

UPDATE: Okay, I've had this app awhile, and I'm less impressed than I was before. The main reason is it keeps screwing up my weight. Sometimes the app won't let me go back a few days and add my weight if I forgot to put it in. I even have a paid membership! I feel so cheated! Also, once I create a recipe, I can't edit or delete it. That irks me a lot! PLEASE fix and update!

I've used the app off and on over the last year, and recently started using it again after not doing so for a few months. Imagine my surprise when I went to scan a food barcode and a function that used to be free was now suddenly a premium feature, and I was asked to pay $39.99 to unlock it? It was nice while it lasted, but nothing about this tracking app is worth even 1/4 of that. I'll be moving on to something more reasonable.

I used this app in college, was free then. I remember it being good. I liked that I didn't have to look up the calories for everything I was eating. After completing the intro quiz, they told me it was $35 a year. That comes $3 a month, which is not that bad. I just dislike that they weren't more upfront about it. I'll just Google how many calories are in a banana for now 🍌 🤷‍♀️

Calorie input is wonky. I'll put in the ingredients as listed on a recipe and the app is showing 50 to a couple hundred calorie difference both ways. Frustrating when you're counting calories to lose weight. I can understand a few here and there but I'm relying on this information to be accurate and it does not appear to be. When you import a recipe from the web, check that the ingredient amounts are correct too. This was another problem I was having.

This was my favorite calorie and exercise tracking app. I understand ad revenue is necessary for free apps but the super loud (even if your phone is on silent!) full-screen ads are not okay. They are like little jump scares since I never know what log might trigger them. The app itself is fantastic. The in-line ads are fine and understandable. These jump scares that ruin my ability to discreetly log my meals are trash. Previous rating was 5 stars.

Tracks you and your information just like any other invasive app. Disappointing that you cannot use the barcode scan feature without giving the app full access to your files and media. This hasnt always been the case. Years ago, when I first used Lose It, it didnt require anything but the camera. I understand that you have to get paid, but dang... Just show a commercial based on workout gear or something. You wouldnt even need to collect user data for that.

I've been able to lose weight, but I can do that with any calorie counting app. Mad that I paid for premium and was maintaining a plan, but I accidentally inputted body fat % into the weight field one day, and suddenly it set my plan from "weight loss" to "maintain weight". There was no way to get the app to go back to "weight loss" mode without starting a new plan. Part of the point for me was seeing the way it tracked my progress, and now it's displaying everything as if I've made no progress

It was nice, but didn't suit my needs. I'm trying to gain weight and it only gave me the option to maintain or lose. So my calorie goal was low and I was surpassing it everyday. Most features require you to upgrade to premium unfortunately. But it has a barcode scanner for easy tracking. Overall it's nice, but not suitable for my needs

I liked the free version of the app, so I paid the yearly subscription. I like the ease of use and it definitely helps me keep my macros in check. However, the app is constantly crashing! I've uninstall/reinstalled several times. Not to mention, it seems to mess with some of my phone's functions. Not sure if I will continue to use the app or if I'll just cancel my subscription, delete the app, and move on.

Banner ads were fine to deal with. I never used anything that is now premium but used to be free, so I can only appreciate how frustrating that would be. However, the addition of unavoidable, timed full-screen adds is definitely pushed me to use another app next time I'm tracking calories closely. Also, a significant number of items are noticabley off per serving. Always verify your own calories.

When it comes to this app I love it. The food database has no limits, I do wish the app was material design based. But there is one thing I'm very concern about. In November I received a message from the app asking me if I would like a discounted lifetime subscription. I accept it, now here in January when my yearly subscription would have been up I was charged an additional $29, which I didn't understand. I called google pay and they told me I would have to contact the developer.

OK but dodgy promotion of premium and filled bad calories/macros so you must research outside the app to make sure you get the right ones. I started with a calorie budget of 1,650. It auto adjusts down as your lose weight. When your calories are under 1,500 it warns you that's too low. But you can't manually set it unless you buy premium. I was offered 51% off premium for 3 days. That expired. Then a day later I was offered 50% off. Pressure tactic, 50% off is the normal price, never pay more

Works well enough and the food database is nice, but I don't need any of the paid features. Unfortunately, the app bothers me multiple times per day with a very intrusive reminder to subscribe. I'd be happy to pay a one time fee of $10-20 to support the app, but since subscribing is the only option, I might try to find another app that bothers me less.

I used to love this app, but now ads have infiltrated it!!! From background banners in between meal periods to full screen ads. Why?! They've also pay-walled the barcode scanner behind the subscription cost. That ain't free anymore, but it used to be and it was SO helpful. Thanks for screwing us over. I'll be looking elsewhere for tracking CICO.

Used to love this app for logging meals and weights. The update added incredibly intrusive ads that pop up whenever you log a food. The premium is way too expensive compared to other apps -- I'm not paying a subscription for an app for $39.99 for 12 months, that's insane. I am just shocked with this update because it is a decent app - just stinks that I started my weight loss journey with this app (and have been very successful, lost over 25 pounds) and I will have to switch to something else.

I love the app but very disappointed in what seems false advertising sale price as $39.99 shows regular and on friends. As I took screen shots of regular price, 25%, and 50% off. The all go to same price point. Other than that I love the app and was going to buy full version until I noticed the sale / non-sale. Also, if it allowed me to pay monthly it would have been even better.

Why is it so hard to find anything in grams?! Literally the easiest and most accurate measurement you can use for logging and only like 10% of food items even give it as an option when logging. Also, many basic featurs that come free with MyFitnessPal are paid-only in this app. Its only strong suits are its less laggy than MFP and the interface is a little bit cleaner.

This app is excellent for tracking what you eat daily, especially if you have BED like me. I used to believe "I wasn't eating much" throughout the day at 250 lbs and counting 6 years ago. Tracking everything down to the ounce truly helped putting things in perspective! Now at 170 lbs, everything is in line and just habit. But! Now the app is ruined with huge unskippable pop-up ads. I get needing to make money, but really!? I don't want to use another app, but I'm contemplating it.

I used to really like this app. Now, features that used to be free (scanning bar code/taking pic of nutrition label to upload product name and nutrition info) are only available to premium members. I'm not even allowed to download the app to my phone so I can have access to it off line. I have to use precious data time and access it through the Play store. Sorry, I used to like the app but, it's too much work now.

This app has been okay to use, but lately it has been having some major database issues, making it frustrating to use. For instance, scanning foods causes items with incomplete nutrition info to appear. There is no way for you to filter out and hide foods with incomplete/ missing nutrition info. And, many foods have incomplete names and brands.

I think this could be a good app if they were to change some things. There are tons of surveys to get through when you first start out before you can even start logging anything. There doesn't seem to be a way to set your own calorie or macro goal for free. If they were to add those as option I think it could be great! I really didn't like that they have plans that have you eating 900 calories a day! Even if they say it's not recommended, it's still irresponsible!

The features are great, and I have had some success in the past with this app. I have recently noted that a majority of the "verified" food products listed in the search are not correct, and are not even in the ballpark of accurate calorie/macro information. This is disappointing. I would expect more accuracy for an app I pay this much for.

Canceled subscription today after more than 2 years of using several times a day to track food. It used to work but hasn't worked right now for a while. Numerous emails with very nice tech support, but the longer I go without this working, the more I no longer have the urge to enter my food in it. After more than 2 years of daily use, I'm done. I now have $30 a year to spend on something else!

Got this app so my family and I could encourage each other. I had done the website before. Such a hassle to try to figure out how to friend someone and to do it its on the website only. Once friends you can't message anyone. It won't let you click on the friend to send a message. Used to love lose it for the social aspect as you lose more weight with friends. So disappointed in the app. Easy logging for weight loss but wanted it for the social it boasts it has. Just not true.

Used to be good, over the years has become less and less user friendly until recently it's gone full corporate and in addition to watching ads after logging an item, you now have to pay for the barcode scanner and most other features that were previously free. Continually stripping features away from free users is understandable as a business model, and if you're determined to lose weight, this shouldnt stop you, but if you're on a regimen, you're honestly better off manually counting calories.

Really? Now I have to sit through popup ads every time I open the app to log food? That's -1 star. The constant barrage of "upgrade to premium" popups whenever I opened the app to log food was bad enough. Also, a lot of food nutrition labels show the servings as cups, tablespoons, etc., but the only options available on the app are grams and ounces. It will also show the servings as "servings" instead of a measurement. If I don't have a nutrition label, how do I know what a serving is?

I wanted to try the free version first. But I am not sure I'll pay for the premium version because of the fairly incomplete macronutrient and the VERY lacking micronutrient databases, even for unprocessed food. I don't need great accuracy for my simple diet, but I expect, at the very least, data on fiber, calcium, vitamin D, iron, etc, for routine health maintenance purpose. Unfortunately, a lot of the values are simply "n/a." So I use this just as a food diary app.

Not impressed. It's ok overall, but annoyed at which features are locked behind pay wall. On the other hand, this is the cheapest pay wall of the trackers I've looked at. Trying to leave MFP; might come back here. UPDATE: went back an hour later, they dropped the discount. I was thinking, "throw some money, check it out", but their bargain is "double the cost if you wait an hour." This ap as expensive as Fatsecret, and Fatsecret has what I need for free.

Probably one of the best tracker apps out there to help you lose weight. That being said I too have been using it for years until as of late due to the paywall change as well. Scanning stuff should not be premium if you want a lot of users to enjoy your app still. What was once free, that was the reason people stayed, shouldn't be put behind a paywall. Don't be like youtube. RIP to this app.

Taking away previously free tools. I have been using this app on and off for years and have really enjoyed using it, however, I opened the app today to put in my meal and went to scan the barcode and I can't. It now has a pop up for premium and is no longer a free feature. This was one of my favorite things to use on the app for convenience, it made tracking so easy. Really disappointed they'd take that away for free users just to try to get people to switch to premium.

Great until the update. I have been using this app for years and one of the main features of this app is now blocked behind a paywall when it used to always be free. There shouldn't be a price on simply scanning items in order to log them vs having to manually type them in order for it to be free. That's a bit outrageous and the main reason I was so motivated to log my meals. Being able to log food however you want shouldn't come with a price at all especially when you're already fairly limited.

i am NOT a paying customer and only use the free options however the application has become increasingly clunky with the pop up ads that inhibit ones entry of foods in the calorie counter. the constant discounts offered of up to 75% off of the price of the app to become a paid user makes me wonder if they are overcharging already for what appears to be a basic calorie counting app that offers no real differentiation from the competition. it does the job but it isnt great and constant pop up ads.

made a rash decision to download this and subscribed to premium yesterday. They have you fill out a refund request form that requires a refund reason, but doesn't have a way to input one.
