Calorie counting can be really dangerous, and they don't have any information about how to try to stay safe about it. I really shouldn't have signed up based on the name itself, but it looked like an okay way to track food patterns. It's not worth the rest of the app to me.

I loved this app. strongly visual and compelling for meal and nutrition tracking for weight loss. i had success! unfortunately it caused my pixel device to malfunction. issues included: No calls in or out, calls would freeze phone, froze and shut down System UI often, inability to play audio or any media, or use the microphone or record audio. i thought it was the phone, but unfortunately it was this app. i would give it more stars but that wasn't my personal experience

I used to love this app, and I have paid for premium, but I can't believe that with a paid membership, I now get regular pop-up ads in the application for glp-1, and other weight loss drugs and or more premium features. I do not need to be inundated with drug ads when trying to track my meals!!!! it almost makes me want to try to get my money back!

After the past 2 updates the recipe logging is borderline unusable as it does not accurately calculate calories per serving because of this. Also, it has started frequently crashing in the background and android is flagging it as a high battery usage app. I loved this to start with as its helped me lose over 50lbs and want to get back to that again.

Basically a less useful version of MyFitnessPal I downloaded this because it was "editors choice." I wanted to like it because I find MyFitnessPal really judgemental with all it's terrible nutrition goals and its unalterable color coding. Lose It is definitely more neutral so that's a win. Unfortunately the take a picture of food feature doesn't really work. What I want is something using advanced image recognition to calculate the food and portion size. I expected less. I got even less.

It was helpful at first so i bought the $30 full version but it never worked for me after that, wouldn't connect to my Fitbit properly. I had to input stuff manually all the time. And it would crash and i would lose my records idk how it happeneded. Many of the food dont even have enough data, I lost all motivation to use it again. I purchased my subscription last June but i dont think i even used it after July. Waste of money. If you're going to use this, just use the free version.

It has a good setup, but it calculates calories wrong which is a big deal for a calorie counting app. It took me a minute to figure out why I would come up with different total calories when I would put in macros. The app calculates fats as 4 cal/gram and carbs as 9 cal/gram which is the exact opposite. That's a big mistake, one that really needs to be corrected. I had higher carb meals calculating crazy high calories and high fat meals super low. Hope this helps someone.

If all you want to do is log calories and use the food database it's fine. I paid for a year membership for premium and none of the links for refipies or meal plans worked, so it felt like a rip-off. Fitness tracker integration is limited. Additionally, I was unable to delete an outlier data point from 2011 (9 years ago!) so none of my current goals tracked properly. More frustrating than helpful. Would not recommend for anything beyond calorie counting.

I installed the app over a year ago. I immediately lost 10 pounds, and another 5 over the first month. I was enthusiastic. I am not fond of the constant pop-up asking me to pay for a premium account. Update:. After using the app for a year and a half, I have uninstall it. The constant pop-ups were annoying, but the last straw was when they made excersize a premium feature. Looking for something new.

Too complicated. It seems to have extra features that some people would like, like lots of nutrients graphics, images of some foods, etc. But I wish I could set my own goals, and I absolutely hate having to answer all these life questions if I haven't used the app in a while. Why are you trying to lose weight? How do you feel? Etc. I just want a simple interface to count calories at my custom amount, maybe log weight, and make sure I get enough protein. I miss my old app that was discontinued.

This is not an ad-supported or free version, but only a free trial. Users should be told up-front, before bothering to download, before the app does its data gathering, that the 'free' version is only a 7 day trial, after which it will automatically charge $39 a year. No scummier than most apps, but that's a low bar. If you're into being manipulated, the graphics look good, and a lot of thought went into the process of making the user feel comfy about it.

I would give this app five stars if it wasn't constantly harassing me to upgrade to premium. The app has a user friendly interface and is an efficient way of food journaling on the go. I love the fact that it can sync with Google Fit and apply my calories burned from my workout to my daily budget. The base form of the app meets my needs but the near constant pop ups urging me to switch to premium are obnoxious. I have no intention of ever getting premium.

I used to love this app. I used this app for years, and I always came back to it after taking breaks from dieting, but it seems like everytime I come back the app gets worse. It's super slow, theres ads everywhere, and everything is behind a paywall now, even the barcode scanner! And premium went up in price! The price it was before was still not worth it for what you get. At this point, I'm better off getting a journal irl and writing down my intake/excercise/weight. This is ridiculous.

Buggy functionalty for logging exercise ('cooking up an update' for 30sec after every single entry for months now), not to mention barcode scanning getting paywalled when it's free on so many other apps. I'm a long-term user of probably a decade and wanted to purchase premium this year. The ads were fine, the loss of free scanning was annoying, but the freezing after exercise inputs is intolerable. I'm a creature of habit and really hate to switch, but LoseIt just isn't functional anymore.

So easy to use and understand. Tracking is totally up to you but by being honest you will achieve your goals. This has helped me lose 50+ pounds and will help me get to my final goal and during maintenance. Recent update has improved scanning of foods so much faster. This is by far the best app I have found for food/exercise/challenge tracking. Please PLEASE fix the scanner. I do not have 5 minutes to wait for the scan.

I used to love this application because of it's fair premium price subscription compared to competitors and it's simple, utilitarian vibe. It honestly helped me lose 70 pounds. But lately it has been crashing on the most important of functions - adding a food. So disappointing that if this isn't fixed, I'm going to have to move to another service. In it's current buggy state, I would not recommend.

Once was great, but has recently started aggressively pushing the premium service with pop-ups. I tried premium for a year, and found that it added nothing special if you're just tracking calories. Now nearly every day I'll press the icon to add something, only to have the premium ad overlay pop up directly where the icon was, and take me to the premium sub page. At this point, I'm too invested with custom recipes and meal history to switch. I get it, you can't develop an app for free, but I'm no

Used happily and successfully for many years. Didn't mind the ads until lately, they are now beyond intrusive, they hinder use of the app. Sadly deleted the app and am here shopping for an alternative. Yes, I tried the paid version for a year and did not need the features and can't justify the cost in my budget. Hoping to find an app that plays nice with Samsung.

I've used this app for years and I really like the usability and interface. It always allowed you to connect your fitness tracker to it to keep track of calories burned. Now all of a sudden you need to have a premium subscription to connect your tracker. Really disappointed and will probably use a different app now that has kept that as a free feature. Shame.

I really wanted to love this app. It's beautifully designed, but to get to micronutrients you have to be premium. Ok. But then when you use the barcode scanner and the only data you get is calories, it's a little disconcerting. The database does have a verified filter, which is good, but there are a lot of discrepancies even when you use that. I guess it's back to MyFitnessPal for me.

I've been using this app an extensive amount of time (6 years) and have found it very useful and easy to log both food and exercises. Recently, pop up ads had been introduced and are extremely intrusive. When adding an item an ad will pop up and only some of the ads are skipable. I completely get that it's in a businesses best interest to monetize but the number of pop ads have made it harder to justify the use of this app. Editing nutrition of an item after scanning it is also broken now.

No love for the forgetful, too fat folks. 💔 I used to weigh in the 100's, now 200's. After tracking for a good stretch, I'll forget and put 100 something instead of 200 something. It will think I've reached my goal. So I have to start all over. I wrote to LoseIt and was told fixing this is low on the list of their developers' priorities, and it is not likely to be fixed anytime soon. I pay for Premium annually. I'm looking around for something better without that flaw. I do not feel seen.

I have used this app for awhile now, it is mediocre at best. The most frustrating thing for me is that if I create an exercise it doesn't stay at the calories I originally input. I know they change because when I create an exercise I input the calories burnt in the title. For some exercises I created awhile back the variance is as high as 10 calories. All together I have noticed subliminal changes that, in my opinion, are done to try and get the user to invest in prime membership.

Not your best choice for counting calories Entering data is easy and intuitive but trying to analyze the data is tedious. Their support articles do a terrible job of explaining how to do something. If you are serious about tracking your macros and understanding which food specifically got you there at the end of the day you'll want a different app.

It looks OK overall, but the foods are usually not listed in grams, which is too annoying. When counting calories, it's easiest to throw the food on a scale and then calculate based on that, rather than trying to figure out tbsp or whatever. Almost every label lists servings in grams, so it should be in the database. Also, the app doesn't seem to let you define a manual calorie target.

Had this exact app years ago. it has changed. I will be looking for a different one. It seems like the just try to make it more and more complex and it doesn't need to be that way, and that's just the free version. there's just more piled on with the paid version. I need to simply record my meald for calorie counting, be able to change things to what I want rather someone else finds my goals unreasonable, that's not for you to decide. and then record my weight. The other things aren't needed.

Good-ish, but has some issues. It says you can adjust calories on the "daily calorie budget screen" by using the adjustment field... but that is not possible because there is no adjustment field. No mention that it is only for the premium service... So frustrating. Often gets stuck searching for a food. Some barcode scans can be adjusted which is helpful, but some are juat plain wrong and I cant adjust. Ugh.

I had downloaded this app and loved it! BUT, I changed phones and had to reinstall it and suddenly to track my protein and carbs, I had to upgrade to premium. I kept the app anyways, just to keep track of calories. Didnt use it much, switched to a different one. Out of curiosity one day, I opened it to check if maybe they allowed carb and protein tracking again, and they not only didn't, they had raised the price! No thanks!

Used this app several years ago to track carbs and meals. Bought the upgraded version even. Extremely annoyed to return to it to discover that most of the useful features (configuring meals per day, carb tracking, etc.) that used to be free are now behind an annual subscription, and no way to just go back to the old version where these things were free. Massively overpriced for annual as well. Greedy. I'll be saying "nope".

Fix your app please. I actually really liked it at first and wanted to purchase premium, but now... 1. The app recently started acting super sluggish and is now frustrating to use. 2. It used to put the foods I've entered before at the top of the search results, but it's not doing that anymore for whatever reason. It takes longer to enter meals now. 3. The ads have gotten overly intrusive. Please vet them better. I've gotten ads that have no obvious way to close them besides restarting the app.

Been using the app for over a year and loved it! I even paid for the subscription. However, as of roughly a week ago, the app keeps crashing whenever I try to open it. I've tried everything I can think of- making a new account, reinstalling the app, clearing the cache and my storage, and even making sure the app is updated, but nothing has worked. Even when I delete and reinstall the app it crashes whenever I try to open it.

Have loved this app but over the past few days, I haven't been able to search for ANY foods. I try to keep up with my calories pretty well so it's unfortunate when the app pretty much just quits working. Doesn't bring up any foods when I search for names or barcodes. I have updated, restarted my phone & the app to no avail. I hope that Lose It! fixes this issue soon!

Used to be good. This app went the way a lot of popular apps do and started inconveniencing it's users in hopes that they'll pay for features that were previously free for years. They took the most used features and put them behind a paywall. You can no longer scan barcodes to log them. Something that's free in almost every single app like this. I used to recommend this app to everyone but I unfortunately have to move on. Yet another service trying to be a subscription. Really disappointing.

I've used Lose It for years now. I have a premium subscription. The most recent update is *terrible*. When you go to add food items to your meal, there is now so much whitespace around everything that only *two items* are visible with the keyboard up. I use My Foods constantly because I can't trust the community items, and it now resets to "all" *every time* (maddening). I regret my lifetime purchase now, it's so much more frustrating to use.

This app is actually the only calorie counting app I like because of its layout. However, why do you have to pay for everything now a days? You used to be able to scan for free, and I really don't mind that because the nutritional value is on the same package anyways, but to have editing the calories payable? Why? It just doesn't make sense, and I'm not mad about it, but I just find it so dumb. Are they going to come to my house and force feed me the remaining calories? Mynetdiary is free tho.

the app has a lot of data points, almost too many. it made navigation difficult. furthermore, syncing with my Samsung health app, steps, and weight was spotty at best. I had to manually enter all my data that is being recorded elsewhere, so it took more time than it was worth to use. I even paid for the premium app and still cosmetic get data to sync properly.

Amazing app that has helped me lose weight and maintain it, but as of late I have noticed ads are starting to get bigger, more obtrusive. I could ignore them at first; however, now I'm getting ads after every meal log and it's way too ridiculous to ignore. That will not make me want to pay for your premium services, but to only delete the app despite have using It for years.

App itself is fine. I use it daily and would have given it 5 stars for being easy to use and having a simple to read UI. I give it 2 because after one of the latest updates, it went from small ads that you could avoid to fill screen unskippable ads. It's clearly predatory. It's not enough that the pay version has tons of features, now they are trying to force it will massive ad vids. My advice is just close the whole app if an ad starts. Otherwise, use something else.

Was a good app for the first week before I realized how difficult it was making things. So many options for the same dishes but each with significantly higher or lower calories, doesn't make sense. I wish it showed the ingredients and measurements, but it doesn't so it's difficult to get an accurate calorie count. Also when I edit the calories for 1 serving. It shows the same calories when I multiply the serving, example after editing 1 serving to be 70 calories, it's still 70 for 4 servings.

Used to be much better. Now the app tries to get you to subscribe to their premium version with every click or opening the app. Gets annoying esp when you're on the run and wanna just input something quickly. Also, they removed calories by macro (protein,carb, fat) and now just leave you with a % so you have to do the math to figure out your macros (now a prem feature). every update = less free features. Going to uninstall this version to replace with an older update I found online. Disappointed

This app has been my favorite food tracking app, but recently the ad banners are very glitchy and continuously bounce between different ads of different sizes, affecting the usability of the app. Because the screen jumps out around due to the different banner sizes, scrolling through to review your food intake of the day has become nearly impossible, and you can only add food through the "+" icon.

This is a great app most of the time; however, the widget that displays calories seldom let's me click on it to add calories. I have to keep the actual app beneath the widget to add calories, otherwise it exits out. Additionally, it links to every other health software excluding Samsung Health. I have a galaxy watch, and I find it rather annoying that I have to manually enter my workouts because the app doesn't allow connection to Samsung Health/products.

I used this program years ago on my computer and really enjoyed it. I reinstalled it today but I can't use it. I'm legally blind and if I have my font sizes turned up on my phone, it cuts text off Lose it or it still keeps much of the text at a default small size. This is just the lastest in a long line of apps with disappointing or non-existent accessibility features.

I loved it at first. Because weight is such a struggle, I started with a smaller goal, with the intention of later adding more. After reaching the first goal, I reset it to my new goal weight. I was prompted to choose "keep data" or "clear data." I chose "keep." My data was still erased. I was paying close attention to specific eating habits and foods, at the direction of a healthcare provider. Now I have lost nearly 2 months of food tracking, taking away valuable information for my next visit.

Very simple to use but, it can get a little tricky at times. I think it would be a much better experience if they added some features such as ... 1) CSV import/export for weight, 2) Sync water consumption with Google Fit (for premium users), 3) Add an app for WearOS smartwatches for quick & easy access, 4) Quick add option so it is faster and more convenient than searching for a specific item.

I've used the free version of this for years and it's always been helpful. I echo others' frustration with the last update but it's the ads that are the worst. I expect ads, of course, but they've become more frequent and some of them can't be closed or backed out of - I have to close and reopen the app to get rid of them. Even worse is the very spammy/scammy nature of some of the ads - alarming "warnings" about your device with instructions for supposedly fixing it. It's inexcusable.

it's still a great app in theory. it's just incredibly glitchy and slow as it freezes constantly. if I didn't own the app I would probably switch to a different platform. edit lowered to 1 star. the app constantly crashes. You can't even log a meal without having to restart the app over and over because of constant crashing. Save your money and go with a better app. I feel bad for the people who were swindled into buying this with a one time purchase.

Edit: the new UI update is sluggish even on a new phone. I don't know what they did, but it sucks. original 5 star review below; Quite an amazing tracker. Syncs with all the fitness wearables. Allows for a LOT of customization for the metrics you want to track/goals you want to set, but it's not too complicated to navigate. Reasonable price when compared to services like Fitbit Premium (which sucks, don't buy Fitbit.)

Used this app a couple of years ago when it let you scan the barcode of the things you eat. No intrusive ads, no frequent, in your face, ads. Now, every other meal you input makes an ad pop up like a mobile game. While I like the app and still use it, I cant recommend it because of how bad the ads are now.

Ive stopped using this app and dont recommend it anymore. I wish I never paid for the premium subscription because at some point they changed how you view your daily log of nutrients. It was once easy to scroll through all of them - now you only get your top 3 to view in your daily log and everything else you have to go into multiple pages. it's inefficient and a huge PITA!.

I don't know why you guys got rid of the little floating + button where you can hit it and start adding entries. I used to use it everyday to log my glucose and my food. Now the button is gone, and I have to fish for where I have to put my glucose entry. I don't know why you would remove functionality from an app that was being used every day.

used to love this app but it has devolved into a piece of trash. when you try to add foods (add foods from yesterday) it doesn't add all even though everything is selected in the menu. other issues as well. trying to add stuff and it's interrupted by poorly paced ads that pop up in weird places. zero stars if it were possible!

Not a fan. Everything good is locked behind premium. You can watch ads to unlock certain features but half of the time the ad doesn't let you exit out, so you have to close the app and try again. They offered premium for 20$ for the year, but now when I try to buy it they want 80$. This is not worth 80$.

I returned to this app- it has way less free features now, not only that, when setting it up, it asks you if you want to add things to your "plan", then at the end of set up tells you that you need to pay to include what you selected when setting up.. Why not be upfront with the fact that it costs money instead of wasting user's time by making them think these things can be included in the plan? Membership seems predatory by the way it is always "on sale" for different prices for different users

I've been using this app for many years and am a premium subscriber. The latest update is horrible, full of bugs, and difficult to navigate. It has lost a lot of my recipes, search is broken, and it won't show many past meals when I'm trying to add food to a new meal. I want to revert to the previous version. if that's not possible and this isn't fixed very soon I'm canceling my membership and finding another app.

I have been logging with this app every day since September 2012. It has worked very well and helped keep my weight loss program on track. UPDATE: 7/11/25. lately the ads are becoming extremely annoying! Once they pop in you need to close and reopen the app. I have been using this app for many years without any problems. Very disappointed to say the least. UPDATE 12/23/25 The ads have become so annoying that you need to close and reopen the app to get back to make entries. APP IS NO GOOD.

Can no longer log in (did try to uninstall and then re-install). When I enter email address for password reset, nothing happens. It's correct email, where I was getting weekly logs sent. App had been glitchy - Google Fit data doesn't sync, water usage could only enter by typing in exact amount. Anyone able to help get logged back in?

This app is absolutely terrible! the onboarding is an endless list of unrelated questions that are frankly overly personal and unnecessary. you are a calorie counting app, you do not need to know who I live with and what I do on my free time. After spending nearly 10 minutes answering question after question, you get to a deadend if you already have an account tied to your email. So you have to start all over in a different flow. this app is not worth my time based on the onboarding alone!

This app could be so good, but it's on the slide towards enshitification, they're making it worse and worse with every update and paywalling features that used to be free, like setting your budget. and if you pay for ad-free, you're still going to get an ad every single day for the premium version. it sucks to see good companies do this to themselves.

The recent update doesn't make any sense. It's harder to see your foods and more complicated to add them for no reason. They also took away being able to see macros at the top unless you pay for premium. With the ridiculous amount of ads I have to watch to use this app, it's not worth it so I'll be deleting this and using something else.

Simple interface no longer. UI has changed so much in the last year or two to be practically unusable on an android phone. I just want to add food an make sure I'm not over for the day, that use to be simple on here, now it's not. Accessibility has also gotten worse as they shrink text to keep trying to cram more stuff onto the main screen. Cancelling premium subscription on it

I've used this app for years. 1600+ day streak of logging weight. I bought the lifetime subscription because I loved it. In the past few months the app has developed terrible input lag. there have also been UI changes that make the simple task of logging calories many more clicks than it was. Give me back the 2023 version!

I own premium. A UI update has made it so that the dashboard at the top of the log ONLY shows the top 3 nutrients you set it to track. I SPECIFICALLY BOUGHT PREMIUM FOR IT TO TRACK FIBER AND SODIUM *ALONGSIDE* MACROS, WHICH IT NOW NO LONGER DOES. It has essentially reverted me back to the free version feature-wise, leaving me feeling cheated considering the cost of premium.

it kind of doesn't.... work? I press buttons and they don't respond, or they produce errors that only go away when I restart. Additionally, the app let me use my account without me verifying my email first, which was strange. I didn't even open the email; it just glitched out, tried to make me go through the whole sign up process again, and when I clicked the back button it logged me in automatically. It was weird and I didn't like it.

Absolutely abysmal new UI I've been a lifetime subscriber for 4 years now. It was an amazing app, the best Id used for tracking. Now its slow and glitchy, loading takes 5 times longer than before, half the time it can't research items and you have to close and reopen to add the rest of your meal. Its limited the amount of macronutrients I can see at the top(seriously?), so you have to change pages and dig for it. I have a sneaking suspicion its to be able to play an ad for the free people.

Tried again because MFP is changing too much. Kept crashing trying to even get my account set back up. Wouldn't let me continue without buying an annual plan and kept going back to start setting up a new plan. 🙄 Missing too much. So many foods from chain places aren't listed. Tried scanner many times on different products and it wouldn't work. Inaccurate nutritional information on various items. Will go back to MFP.

I used this app for about a year consistently. I used the basic version which didnt include scanning or some other detailed features. As time went on there were more ads but I understand they need to make money from the basic version but the recent update where you can't see your macros and can only see calories was too much for me. macros are the foundation of healthy weight loss and hiding that basic necessity behind a pay wall is infuriating. make it a paid app at this point its useless now.

I don't want my health data stored in the cloud. I don't want an account, I just want to track calories on my local device. If you forced me to make an account from the start maybe that would be fine, but the fact that you made the app look usable without an account and wasted my time entering a bunch of data in order to trick me into creating an account afterwards is deceptive and disrespectful. I won't support a company which uses dark patterns.

I have had a lifetime membership for a few years now. With the total change of the header that is at the top of your daily log, I am no longer interested in using this app. The development team seems to think that reducing the number of items viewable at a glance to three at a time is awesome. Previously, I could check my Total Calories,Protein,Fat,Carbohydrates,Cholesterol,Net Carbohydrates and fiber,all at the top of the screen. But now I can only pick three to see in a semi-circle. Lame!

I've had this app for about 2 years and at first it was amazing, but now everything is only unlocked with "premium" and projections aren't given until a 7 day streak. The original weight loss projections were accurate without waiting. I have lost a lot of weight, but I'm not paying monthly for things on this app that used to be free. Definitely going to find a better app!

I've had Lose It for years but in the last few months they have increased the number if pop ups and ads to the point where every click you get a new ad. Not only that, but now they are taking previously free features (barcode scanner) and locking them behind a pay wall. I understand that apps need to make a profit, but at this point it's just greed. I would have been more likely to buy membership if they were developing new advanced features, but taking free features is ridiculous.

The app is just "okay". My biggest issue is how it does NOT usually any any information on vitamins and nutrition aside from macros and calories. This includes even when scanning the bar code on the label. I've spent time adding information, looking it up on usda website but honestly, I paid for a lifetime membership, they should have that already. I don't have to do that with MyFitness Pal, it's already there and free. So I paid to essentially have to work for them?

Purchased lifetime since I'd been using the app for years at this point. Functionality is great, very useful, only ever had one hiccup that got resolved in a later patch anyway. After the latest update, I am getting ads for a 3rd party keto diet course that no matter how many times I say no thanks, it will not go away. It is very annoying to have a life time membership and STILL get ads, for a service I don't want, from a company that isn't LoseIt, under a membership that says I don't get ads.

Update: It was working better for a few months, but now the bar code scanner and internet connection issues are back. I've used the app to off and on for years, but recently it won't load search results. It keeps stating that there is no internet connection. It happens if I'm on mobile data (with full bars) or WiFi. It's extremely frustrating. Calorie logging in useless if you can't find half the items you need. What's even more frustrating is that I pay for premium and it doesn't work.

Been using this for a couple years. It was working great until about the last 3 months, now they're always pushing updates that seem to make it worse. The latest update effectively ruined the ability to create your own recipes. When you go in to input the total weight/servings for the recipe, instead of dividing the total calories by the weight for the servings, it now multiplies it. That's one of several bugs that have been creeping in lately where before everything worked perfectly.

Regularly brought my battery down massively!!!! Tried this for a few weeks and liked the interface and I managed to keep it up for a while. But then I noticed that my battery was being dramatically drained. So I started the process of elimination and deleted the most recent apps I downloaded one at a time. And it turns out this app was the culprit. By the end of the day I usually expect my battery to be about 60% with this app it was dropping to 30%. Shame on these devs, shouldn't happen.

I had a lot of success with this app last year after moving to it from MyFitnessPal. I left that app because it started charging for the barcode scanner. Fast forward to today and I set it up for another round of weight loss and to my surprise they too have moved the barcode scanner to the paid section. I don't mind ads, but I'm not paying yearly for an app when free alternatives exist. I wouldn't mind paying an upfront price, but none of the features behind it's paywall are worth a subscribe

Terrible experience. The app frequently hangs and crashes, barcode scanning only works like 5% of the time, and pretty much everything is locked behind a premium subscription at this point. Worst of all there's ads CONSTANTLY. Every time you add food to a meal, it plays a full screen ad for 10-15 seconds. Plus a bunch of "Verified" foods are just completely incorrect. Don't bother using the app, it used to be decent but I'd pretty hostile to use now. Any other calorie counter is better than this

This is a great app easy to use. Customizable for your needs. And best of all it free and can be upgraded. Every food I eat is in it. The bar code scanner makes for easy entry of all food. You can copy previous meals to save time. update I purchased the life time plan and now years later after charging me $40 I'm suddenly down graded and they want me to pay a monthly premium. learn from my loss do not use this product or buy thier services. Google has a free app that does the same.

The app had me go through several questions for initial start up. I gave it my weight, height, goals, and it gave me a daily calorie limit. Once that was done, it loaded up the app... Only for it to have all my numbers wrong, answered questions wrong, goals wrong, and the timeline suddenly being limited to one year unlike two years in the start up questions.

I've been a frequent user of Lose It throughout the years. Even paid for premium a few times. I'd just planned to start using it again only to find out that scanning barcodes is now a paid feature. Uninstalled the app on the spot. I'm not paying for a standard, basic feature—especially one that gives you incorrect nutritional info 95% of the time.

I really like the app, but it lacks the level of integration similar apps have. I want a premium app that integrates with samsung health, but myfitnesspal is too expensive and doesn't offer a lifetime membership. This app is almost the same price for the subscription but does offer a lifetime subscription. Unfortunately, it lacks Samsung Health integration. In fact, it offers very little integration compared to MFP.

I used to use this app because it was simple and easy to read and use. Now it's complicated, ugly interface, and EVERY click leads you to an ad for premium service, and also shows you features that are locked for non-payers. If it was a one time fee, I'd consider it. But it's a sub so forget it. Get off my phone. I'm replacing it with something else.

Loose it was recommend to me because of its accuracy of nutritional facts. It's not accurate. Tracked calories for years. My fitness pal "free" is more accurate. Probably others as well but I can't vouch for them. After a few weeks this app starts sending lots of ads & stops you from inputting your data as well. Looking forward to "loosing this app" & going back to something that is effective for my fitness goals.

Very frustrating to use. The database that this app draws from doesn't agree with the label on the package. In most cases you actually consume more calories than you think. Correcting the numbers is very difficult. I thought this app would save time counting calories but it has increased it a significant amount, while reducing the accuracy. Everywhere you look "get premium and this feature will work", can't even track exercise without paying $40. This app is more trouble than it's worth... FREE.

I've used lose it for a long time now and preferred it to mfp, but I got a new phone and forgot to install it for a little bit after and when I did now you can't even customize your BASIC calorie allowance! You can, but you've got to PAY PAY PAY! Don't get me wrong, I understand you gotta make money and gotta paywall a lot of stuff, but a fixed budget is the most basic feature. I don't want to consume 2500 calories a day, I would prefer to set to lower number. Will be sadly Uninstalling.

Was a Great app this was my review before they locked everything behind a pay wall. Now it's a useless app and not worth the hassle. love that most of the best features are free. It's great that I can share my meals with my wife who is also tracking and eating pretty close to the same thing. You can create recipes as well which really helps as most of the healthiest meals are going to be cooked from scratch instead of from a box.

This app makes me sad now. I've used this app on and off for many years now. It used to be a lot better. Since the addition of ads the app is very slow and clunky. I used to zip through and add up all my food in no time but now it it's slow and frustrating. At least they're getting paid I guess. It's always at the expense of the user experience though. Just about like every other app and service I use, the product gets worse as the prices go up and you get nickel and dimed to death.

I used to use this app it WAS very useful. But now with all the pop up ads. It freezes. Is inaccurate and simply just sucks. Uninstalled and reinstalled but to no avail. Some things are better left alone. But being an app that is a cash grabber I too will moving to another app. It's sad really because I had been using this app for years. It was easy to track with the scanner option but now it costs. I was a premium member but it had nothing to offer for the price, so I went back to basic.

I've been using this app for at least 10 years. Some time ago, I decided to agree to pay $100 for lifetime premium. Looking back, not really worth it. VERY, very few "verified foods" and those that exist aren't accurate. App is very buggy when a user tries to edit (correct) erroneous nutrition data. Now, there is a notification that can't be cleared unless the user pays $20 for a useless education program. RUN - don't walk - to MyFitnessPal or Weight Watchers.

I used this app (free version) over the years on and off. This time, they want money for the barcode scanner. I will never pay a subscription for an app. Maybe a one-time fee of 2 dollars or something (to get the scanner and basic app) but subscriptions are against my app philosophy. No thanks. I'll go somewhere else. Another customer lost!

I tried the free app and it didn't allow me to post my meals. In fact I wasn't able to find the daily journal or start a food list. It continually informed me that I had to buy the upgrade to make it work. I have used other free diet apps over the years, putting up with the adds and they worked fine. This app, like the other new ones is too busy (if it worked). I am disappointed and shut it down.

This app took away features from the free version that have been there for years and now require payment for premium subscription in order to access the same stuff we could before. This app had the barcode scanner for free for years and now they want you to buy the premium subscription in order to use it. Honestly, that is just annoying. They just want as much money as they can get. This feature was literally free for they past like 5 years. This was my go too app but not anymore.

Update 2/21/24 Won't import recipes from the web. Just gives an error message. Also won't give results when searching for recipes I haven't logged yet. Must be some kind of bug, but there's no way to report it. There's so much this app is lacking in terms of functionality. Really wished it synced with Google or Samsung health. Also printing out a food log is a pain, especially since I just want the food portion and nothing else. It could use some work but it's cheaper than others so 🤷.

I was trying to decide between this and another free calorie app (netdiary). I kind of liked this one better, until the full page ads started popping up when I was right in the middle of logging meals. I understand having ads in a free app, but do they really need to be full page, comletely interrupting app use? Whenever I tried to "X" out of the full page ads the app would freeze, so I would close it out and when I went back whatever I was in the process of logging was lost. Frustrating!

I've been a user of this app off and on for years. Now barcode scanning is a premium feature?!?! Thanks but no thanks! I thought about going premium when I last started up just before the new year when scanning was free, now I most definitely will NOT pay for something I was getting for free. The content is user-added and not always accurate. NOW you have to pay to enter accurate data for the app?! Nah! I'm not paying so I can do what I'm paying for the app to do.

App actually works great. Track calories, plan meals, track progress, etc, and it comes with a lot of features to help you on your journey. The one star is due to how unsafe this app is. There's a lot of disgusting men that randomly send friend requests or messages, and harass users. Many request money. Lose It team refuses to do anything about it. There's no block feature. Use app at your own risk.

Originally I would have given this app 5 stars. I've been using it for 5 years and loved it. Now they have added huge pop up ads that play sound for multiple seconds so you cannot even close them right away. If you're going to have giant pop up ads, at least have them auto-mute. Some people log food in the office, at restaurants, etc. and would prefer not to force others around them to listen to an ad about knee pain.

When I first got this app a little over a year ago, it was great. Logging my foods was quick and easy! Now, logging foods has become slow and tedious because the app has changed too many tools to only be available with premium. Speaking of premium, the price is ridiculous for anyone using the app. This app is not special in any way for the price to be what it is. In my opinion i would not waste my time with this app and i would encourage you into finding a better app to use instead of this one.

I was a huge fan and long term user: I liked that this app hits the sweet spot of tracking what I want to track, just the facts, no guilt. As happens sometimes, I lapsed in meal tracking for a couple months. When I came back, LoseIt blocked my access to tracking behind a wizard that guilted me for lapsing and made me write a paragraph about "what would be different this time." If I'm paying a subscription, then the core product (tracking) should be available, always, with zero friction.

Most frustrating app I've used. After going through set up and beginning my first food diary by adding my lunch, every item was added twice and I wasn't able to delete it once it was submitted. I did notice it before and checked all the doubles off then submitted it but it kept them all. I just want an easy, simple and no drama app. This wasn't it for me.

After 8 days I'm finding a different app. It seems to have issues with basic math. Edit; leave a negative review and they will delete your account. Basic math issues; adding food, it tells you the macros and then adds whatever it wants. I watched my goal date change every time I opened the app. Left 2 stars, changed it to one after discovering they deleted my account.

Shady cancellation practices. I wanted to cancel my yearly auto renew. The app tells me to log into the website by clicking 'login at top right'. That option isn't there, nothing is in the top right. Clicking the 'become a member or log in' opens up Google play to install the app. I've been clicking around the website for ten minutes and can't find a way to log in that doesn't direct me to the app, which I can't cancel the subscription from. This would have been a positive review otherwise.

Latest update wiped out the last 29 months of data after I declined a two-month trial subscription to the premium plan. I reached my present/ideal weight 10 years ago using LoseIt and now use it to track what I eat and record recipes, so losing all this data is really disappointing. It froze as I tried to log what I ate for dinner. eta: After all these years of adding items to the database, I'm being prompted to join premium to scan in bar codes. This is what ensh!ttification looks like.

Edit on 10/23/23 Having issues today and getting customer service is arduous to say the least. They emailed me. I responded and an hour has gone by with no response from them. Very frustrating. This kind of customer service is no appropriate. I had to uninstall the app. I reinstalled and it lost all my info. Not even realizing I'm a premium member. All of their fixes don't work! At this point I just want my money back. I'm over it.

App is awesome. I'm down 18 pounds in 46 days. Looking forward to losing more in the future. Update... I'm 143 days in and I just hit 35 lbs down and 5 inches in my pants. This app is amazing if you're diligent and stick to it. Edit: The addition of ads have made the app much more difficult to use. I'm now looking for another alternative. It's been great but this is a deal breaker.

I've been using this since the barcode fiasco with the other app, and I even pay to support the developer. Recently a pop up ad has started for something called Nutrisense, and it needs to stop. I'm not paying for no ads, just to get sponsored pop up ads. If it continues I'll just drop the app and move on to the next one like I've done in the past.

For a long time, I held this as a five-star as it was one of the best apps I've used. With a recent update, they have restricted some of the things that made it the best. One such thing is the scanning of the barcodes to input food is now only allowed to be used if you're paying for a subscription. At $10 a month, when I am just using the app to help track food, that seems unreasonable. It was a fantastic app, but as time has passed It has become very limited and less practical.

Buggy and annoying. It asked me to reset my goals & weight pretty much every time I booted it up. I had this app for a while, and it didn't start out this way, but over time, it started losing meals I had input. It also became harder to find pretty standard foods via the barcode scanner, and I noticed there was little regulation on the nutrition info on existing foods. I also hate that all of the email marketing shifted to GLP-1 promos. Not good for general nutrition tracking.

Literally doesn't even work. Asks you a bunch of questions that probably mostly only apply to paid users (and are just stealing your information to sell off), then tries to update 'this won't take long' it says, as it wastes my entire 30 break. Even tried un and reinstalling. If it's like I remember it it's bloated anyways. I just want to track my calories.

Ive been using this app for about a year and liked it more than MyFitnessPal at first, but not anymore. Now it has so many ads. Every time I log in an item, I get an ad. I understand developers need their money somehow, but this is a ridiculous amount and makes using the app so frustrating. They also removed the barcode scanner feature unless you pay for premium. Just another money-hungry company.

Very disappointed. I was using this app to track my nutrient intake (such as sodium). It was working very well, then I had issues with my phone and had to uninstall several apps. When I re-installed the app about a month later the tracking part of the app that I had previously used was no longer available on the app. Uninstalled again since I need more than just calorie counting.

Horrible app! It automatically charges you $39.99 for a yearly subscription after advertising a 7-day free trial. Moreover, it overwrote the existing data I entered in the initial questionnaire, identified me as female, messed up my DOB, and allocated me just 555 calories a day (ridiculous!). I just wasted 30 minutes on a Sunday evening answering their questionnaire, which I thought was going to get me somewhere.

I hate how several features got locked behind a paywall while nothing new was added. The main feature that made this app helpful was the ability to scan the barcode for food, but that got taken away. I deleted my account when I found that out. It didn't help they're selling your personal data as well as trying to get you to pay for features that used to be free

Was very excited, until I tried to plan out the foods in my lunch for the next day. Even LOOKING at the next day requires premium membership? I write out what I eat BEFORE I eat it to make sure I don't blow my calorie budget? Of all the things to put behind a paywall, that's what you choose? I downloaded this because I had finally given up on MyFitnessPal, guess LoseIt is just as bad! Fastest app I ever uninstalled!!

I loved this app for so long, but now it's becoming nothing more than just another calorie counting app. Previously free features are now locked behind a paywall, seriously? Features I used just a few months ago I can no longer use unless I want to be a 'Premium' user. That's exactly why I swapped from My Fitness Pal to Lose It in the first place. Guess it's off to find a new app once again. ✌🏻

Very detailed and invasive questionnaire just to open the app and create an account. Then the 7 day free trial is automatically connected as a google play subscription that will automatically get paid in 7 days, unless you directly go to google play and cancel the subscription. This is VERY sketchy behavior. May be a useful app, but this put me off completely. Account deleted. No thanks.

No longer tracking the number of days until I reach my weight loss goal. Each day when I add in my calories consumed and hit done. It show "0" days instead of the number of days that I have been using this app. It also no longer tells me the date that I will reach my goal. It also no longer syncs to my fossil smartwatch & Google fit app. Very disappointing..... I'm going to look for another app.

Would prefer to give 0 stars. Been logging on this app for over 1035 days and up until 3pm today everything was fine. Annoying with the intrusive ads after every log, but fine for a free app. Logged in only to find that all of my progress and information was wiped and I have to take a quiz with detailed personal information to open the thing. Over two years and 20lbs gone for what? I'm out. Ads had me on the fence but this tipped it. Don't waste your time on this app.

I can't recommend the app, anymore. The new privacy policy fully admits to selling my data to 3rd parties. I purchased the full prices app for $80 to stop the ads. Now I get ads for prescriptions from a virtual DR who would know nothing about my history. Also new users can't purchase the app like I did, and have 2\3 of the functions, like goals or even basic water tracking is locked behind a monthly subscription pay wall. 0 stars if I could.

WARNING: Pop-Up ads will take over your phone! Ad music plays the entire time I am using the app, even if an ad does not pop up. Lately it's been the music for the awful Star Trek ad, but it changes. The music will continue to play until I close the app. If I exit the app but don't close it, the music plays over everything else on my phone. It happens every time I use the app, making it annoying and useless.

-For a couple years now I'd rate this 5 STARS, one of the most reliable and nicely set up apps, I love how it looks and functions. BUT that changes now, because in the last few months they've taken a sharp greedy downturn and thrown the users under the bus; first it started with pushing pop ups rarely to sign up for their premium. Used to be just an unobtrusive small banner ads at bottom. But NOW, sadly, when you finish logging a meal most times and don't have prem, a FULL SCREEN ad, unskippable

Edit: Long time user here (years). I do not need and would not use the premium features, so I've always had the free version and did not mind the banner ads. The recent addition of full screen pop-up adds (with sound!) is slimy and very obtrusive. I can no longer recommend this app to friends and family, and I will be looking for something else myself. I might be willing to pay a small one-time fee for an ad free version, but a premium subscription would never be worth it to me.

This use to be an awesome free app. Now that they have put all the features behind a paywall, it is only good for calorie counting alone. There are better apps out there, especially if you are already using a fit bit or other movement tracker. I would be fine paying a one-time charge to upgrade, but not a monthly/yearly subscription. I'll find something else.

There are a lot of food selections, that makes calorie tracking easy for the most part. It does not integrate with my watch or Google fit properly. It consistently is extending my goal day out, no matter if I am eating half of my calories. It doesn't consistently save my weight, so every few days it records me back at my starting weight messing up my projection. I even paid for the premium subscription because I was recommended it so heavily. I have cancelled it after using for six months. BAD!

I've used lose it off and on for years. I just reinstalled it and found out that I can no longer scan barcodes unless I want to go premium. It's one thing to pay for some features that make the experience more in depth and desirable but how can one even use an app such as this without scan feature is beyond me. It would be more hassle then the app is helpful. It should be included in basic plan. Please note, if money is not an issue for you, use it. Its a good app! Basic isn't non functionable.

I've used this app for about 4 years daily. Up until recently it was a fantastic app that I would recommend for others to track calories, enter recipes, and set nutrition goals. I even purchased the premium version of the app a few years back. The pop up ads recently have made this almost unusable and beyond annoying. I'm now shopping around to find an alternative as this is flawed. It is a shame that the developers desire for a cash grab have ruined this once great app. Beyond disappointed.

Can't try it without giving credit card info. It was suggested that I try this app, so I installed it and agreed to providing my credit card. After playing around with it, I see it's really no different than the free apps I already use and not as comprehensive. An example: I scanned Eggo's barcode into MyFitnessPal (free version) and it comes right up, this app doesn't recognize it. Lo and behold I can't find out how to cancel before they charge my credit card, they've hidden it very well.

A year ago, when I began my fitness journey I absolutely loved Lose It! I would have sworn by it and given it 5 or more stars. It was user-friendly and helped me stay on track with reminders and the scanner feature that allowed me to scan items to stay within my goals. Now, you need to pay for a feature that has always been free. I decided to uninstall the app and look for something more suitable for my needs. Sad that an app that was so helpful decided to take away such a useful tool for money.

I've used the free version on & off since 2014. Last year, I upgraded to the paid version. Everything was great, all options unlocked, no ads. A breeze to use. Since last week when my annual membership expired, at random moments I've been getting full-screen ads (which you are held hostage to view in their entirety). I found an option where I can pay $9 to rid my app of all ads, for life. But for a $9 ransom, you can use the app normally. How about using banner ads??????

I've never written a negative review. And I didn't think my first would be for lose it! But as others have been mentioning lately, the ads have gone over the top. I'm currently on a 842 day streak. The app itself is great, but over the years since my initial use of it, I've seen the ads slowly become more intrusive. It's at the point now I have no choice but to switch back to MyFitnessPal. Half the time, trying to click out of the ad actually opens it. You have to completely exit the app.

Downloaded this app and chose the free basic version rather than the $39.99 a year version with a 7 day trial period. Then it offered me a 25% discount. So I accepted that so I could try it with all of it's benefits and see how I liked it. Within an hour or two I hated it. I'm pretty app savvy and I found the UI frustratingly counterintuitive. When I tried to cancel, it said it would not renew my subscription in 12/2020! I guess the discounted price didn't include the 7 day trial. Unacceptable!!

I do not like the changes being made to this app. Was easy to see previous weeks, or even push of the button and scrolls back a week and more weeks if necessary, now have to enter a date. Now the free version has taken away the barcode scanner. It had been included with the original contract when I signed up. This is a breach of contract and a class action law suit should be filed. The cost is way too high to switch to premium version. I will search for another ap. It is a shame.

I used to love this app and recommend it. In the last 3 weeks the pop up ads have gotten so frequent I can't stand it anymore. On top of that, the ads have become videos that you CANNOT stop and close. That was it for me. I understand the ad supported model, but it's now crossed the line to be so annoying I am moving to a competitor. Edit - the auto-bot reply was how to pay to stop the ads. It's clear they do not value the customer experience unless you pay. Too bad.

Used this app for quite a while, but finally had to give up on it. It now asks you to buy the full version every single time you open it back up. The app just freezes for several seconds and you start to think your phone is frozen, but nope, the app is just loading it's "do you want the full version" dialog box...

This was my app of choice for about 4 years for counting calories and macros. The changes recently have made it more and more difficult to use the free version. Now you have to watch videos just to add food, or pay a subscription. I will NOT be purchasing the premium version that you try heavily to steer users towards, but I will now be finding another app. Disappointed in the change.

I am a premium subscriber. I give the app 1 star. It tracks calories and macros, but it's an open source database that is created by users, so the data is often incorrect or missing. There are no micronutrients listed to see if your intake is adequate. The fasting and calorie flexibility is great. The message board is horrible, they don't monitor it and it's full of spam and children. I won't be giving them anymore money. It's a waste of an app.

I apparently gave this app five stars back in 2015, but now I'm lowering it to a 1. This is a great app usability-wise, but now they have full-screen, loud, video ads. I know the idea is to use full screen ads to hopefully annoy users enough to get premium, and if not hey at least you earn extra money because the video ads pay better, but you're just going to push them away instead. MyFitnessPal integrates better with other apps anyway so this could be just the kick I need to make the jump.

Not worth a damn. This app used to be free. Came back several years ago then it had some options to pay for and one free option spammed with ads. Now come back a third time and there is no free option. Not interested. Gonna look into other apps. I could do this pen and paper for free I guess if they all cost charge monthly now. Who needs another annoying bill when you're just trying to do better for yourself? Lame.

I used to love the app as it helped me. The problem is that it locks many features behind a paywall that it wants you to purchase several times when using the app in the form of fullscreen pop-ups. I was able to ignore this as I only needed to count calories. Recently, however, in an effort to either make more money, or drive off users, the developer(s) have locked a once free feature behind the premium paywall. Adding foods via barcode must have been too convenient as you can no longer do it

Great app on keeping up with what you eat in addition to being able to add foods that are not already in database. Update on review 12/30/24. They took away a lot of the items that made a great free app and now charge for them. One being you can't scan in new food or add recipes that you use. It's almost useless now as a free app. I guess the ads weren't enough income.

It works the same as every other calorie counter on here. Ultimately uninstalled because the endless pop-up ads trying to coerce me into buying premium are not only annoying but also majorly slowed down the app. EDIT: downgraded to one star because the automated "customer service" reply is a complete joke and just shows that all they care about is lining their pockets. Next time, try programming your bot to say a variety of phrases.

tried this app out, editors Choice 4.4 Stars. at first it worked great! Easy to find and login food scan a barcode and it shows up. but then if you really want to get serious, and add a fitness Tracker app, like Fitbit. lose it wants to charge you $30 just to connect the two. Every other calorie counting app that I checked out, my fitness pal, Sparkle Etc all let you connect for free. but not this one if you want to connect one of only two fitness trackers it's $30 up front.

Updated 10.13.24: So doubling down on the intrusive ads, the "done logging" feature of the app only works after you have been fed and ad. This means that if you log all day getting ads and then open the app just to say you are done, you cannot save this interaction. Terrible. 0 stars if I could I do not mind ads in an app, but they have gotten very intrusive lately popping up right where you are going to click. Needless to say, that is not good design and only makes me think of uninstalling.

The calorie counting system is inaccurate. A gram of carbohydrate contains 4 calories. A gram of protein also contains 4 calories. A gram of fat, though, contains 9 calories. This system doesn't count calories that way. They say a medium banana is 200 calories, when it should be closer to 100. I plugged in the calories and macros for my protein powder and it told me that I plugged in the calories wrong. No, their system is whacked out and doesn't make any sense.
